Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: for any of the fifth qualifying period to the seventh qualifying period, the result (expressed as a percentage) of the formula

Subsection.Text: The portion of paragraph (b) of the definition top-up revenue reduction percentage in subsection 125.7(1) of the Act before subparagraph (i) is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: for any of the eighth qualifying period to the tenth qualifying period, the greater of

Subsection.Text: Paragraph (c) of the definition top-up revenue reduction percentage in subsection 125.7(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: for the eleventh qualifying period and each subsequent qualifying period, the eligible entity’s revenue reduction percentage for the qualifying period. (pourcentage compensatoire de baisse de revenue)

Subsection.Text: Subsection 125.7(1) of the Act is amended by adding the following in alphabetical order:

Definition.Text: executive compensation repayment amount, of an eligible entity, means

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: nil, unless

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: shares of the capital stock of the eligible entity are listed or traded on a stock exchange or other public market, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the eligible entity is controlled by a corporation described in subparagraph (i); and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: if the conditions in subparagraph (a)(i) or (ii) are met, the amount determined by the formula

FormulaDefinition.Text: is

FormulaParagraph.Label: (i)
FormulaParagraph.Text: a percentage assigned to the eligible entity under an agreement if

FormulaParagraph.Label: (A)
FormulaParagraph.Text: the agreement is entered into by

FormulaParagraph.Label: (I)
FormulaParagraph.Text: the eligible entity,

FormulaParagraph.Label: (II)
FormulaParagraph.Text: an eligible entity, shares of the capital stock of which are listed or traded on a stock exchange or other public market, that controls the eligible entity (referred to in this definition as the “public parent corporation”), if the public parent corporation received a deemed overpayment under subsection (2) in respect of the seventeenth qualifying period or any subsequent qualifying period, and

FormulaParagraph.Label: (III)
FormulaParagraph.Text: each other eligible entity that received a deemed overpayment under subsection (2) in respect of the seventeenth qualifying period or any subsequent qualifying period and was controlled in that period by the eligible entity or the public parent corporation, if any,

FormulaParagraph.Label: (B)
FormulaParagraph.Text: the agreement is filed in prescribed form and manner with the Minister,

FormulaParagraph.Label: (C)
FormulaParagraph.Text: the agreement assigns, for the purposes of this definition, a percentage in respect of each eligible entity referred to in clause (A) of this description,

FormulaParagraph.Label: (D)
FormulaParagraph.Text: the total of all the percentages assigned under the agreement equals 100%, and

FormulaParagraph.Label: (E)
FormulaParagraph.Text: the percentage allocated to any eligible entity under the agreement would not result in an amount allocated to the eligible entity in excess of the total of all amounts of deemed overpayments of the eligible entity under subsection (2) for the seventeenth qualifying period and any subsequent qualifying period, and

FormulaParagraph.Label: (ii)
FormulaParagraph.Text: in any other case, 100%, and

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the lesser of

FormulaParagraph.Label: (i)
FormulaParagraph.Text: the total of all amounts each of which is an amount of a deemed overpayment under subsection (2) for each of the eligible entities described in clause (i)(A) of the description of A for the seventeenth qualifying period and each subsequent qualifying period, other than amounts in respect of employees on leave with pay, and

FormulaParagraph.Label: (ii)
FormulaParagraph.Text: the amount determined by the formula

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the executive remuneration of the eligible entity, or of the public parent corporation that controls the eligible entity, if any, for the 2021 calendar year (prorated based upon the number of days of the eligible entity’s, or the public parent corporation’s, fiscal periods in the calendar year, if those fiscal periods are not the calendar year), and

is the executive remuneration of the eligible entity, or of the public parent corporation that controls the eligible entity, if any, for the 2019 calendar year (prorated based upon the number of days of the eligible entity’s, or the public parent corporation’s, fiscal periods in the calendar year, if those fiscal periods are not the calendar year). (montant du remboursement de la rémunération de la haute direction)

Definition.Text: executive remuneration, of an eligible entity, means

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the total amount of compensation that is reported in the eligible entity’s Statement of Executive Compensation for Named Executive Officers pursuant to National Instrument 51-102 Continuous Disclosure Obligations, as amended from time to time, of the Canadian Securities Administrators in respect of Named Executive Officers of the eligible entity;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: if paragraph (a) does not apply and the eligible entity is required to make a similar disclosure to shareholders under the laws of another jurisdiction, the amount of total compensation reported in that disclosure (if the compensation of more than five individuals is required to be reported under that disclosure, using the five most highly compensated of those individuals); and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: if paragraphs (a) and (b) do not apply, the amount that would be required to be reported by the eligible entity using the methodology for preparing the Statement of Executive Compensation referred to in paragraph (a). (rémunération de la haute direction)

Definition.Text: qualifying recovery entity, for a qualifying period, means an eligible entity that meets the following conditions:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: it files an application with the Minister in respect of the qualifying period in prescribed form and manner no later than 180 days after the end of the qualifying period;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: it is a qualifying entity for the qualifying period;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: if it is a corporation (other than a corporation that is exempt from tax under this Part), it

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: is a Canadian-controlled private corporation, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: would be a Canadian-controlled private corporation absent the application of subsection 136(1);

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: if it is a partnership, throughout the qualifying period it is the case that

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the total of all amounts, each of which is the fair market value of an interest in the partnership held — directly or indirectly, through one or more partnerships — by

FormulaParagraph.Label: (i)
FormulaParagraph.Text: a person or partnership other than an eligible entity, or

FormulaParagraph.Label: (ii)
FormulaParagraph.Text: a corporation, other than a corporation that

FormulaParagraph.Label: (A)
FormulaParagraph.Text: is exempt from tax under this Part, or

FormulaParagraph.Label: (B)
FormulaParagraph.Text: is described in subparagraph (c)(i) or (ii), and

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the total fair market value of all interests in the partnership; and

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: it has a revenue reduction percentage

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: greater than 0%, if it is the seventeenth qualifying period, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: greater than 10%, if it is any of the eighteenth qualifying period to the twenty-second qualifying period. (entité de relance admissible)

Definition.Text: recovery wage subsidy rate, for a qualifying period, means

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: for any of the seventeenth qualifying period to the nineteenth qualifying period, 50%;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: for the twentieth qualifying period, 40%;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: for the twenty-first qualifying period, 30%; and

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: for the twenty-second qualifying period, 20%. (taux de subvention salariale de relance)

Definition.Text: total base period remuneration, of an eligible entity, means the total of all amounts, each of which is for an eligible employee in respect of a week in the fourteenth qualifying period, equal to the least of

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the eligible remuneration paid to the eligible employee in respect of the week,

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: if the eligible employee does not deal at arm’s length with the eligible entity in the qualifying period, the baseline remuneration in respect of the eligible employee determined for that week, and

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: if the eligible employee is on leave with pay in the week, nil. (rémunération totale de la période de base)

Definition.Text: total current period remuneration, of an eligible entity for a qualifying period, means the total of all amounts, each of which is for an eligible employee in respect of a week in the qualifying period, equal to the least of

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the eligible remuneration paid to the eligible employee in respect of the week,

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: if the eligible employee does not deal at arm’s length with the eligible entity in the qualifying period, the baseline remuneration in respect of the eligible employee determined for that week, and

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: if the eligible employee is on leave with pay in the week, nil. (rémunération totale de la période actuelle)

Subsection.Text: Subsection 125.7(3) of the Act is replaced by the following:

For a qualifying recovery entity for a qualifying period, an overpayment on account of the qualifying entity’s liability under this Part for the taxation year in which the qualifying period ends is deemed to have arisen during the qualifying period in an amount determined by the formula

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the recovery wage subsidy rate for the qualifying period;
is the qualifying recovery entity’s total current period remuneration for the qualifying period; and
is the qualifying recovery entity’s total base period remuneration.

Subsection.Text: For the purposes of this Act other than this section, and for greater certainty, an amount that an eligible entity is deemed under any of subsections (2) to (2.2) to have overpaid is assistance received by it from a government immediately before the end of the qualifying period to which it relates.
Paragraph 125.7(4.2)(d) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: if the seller meets any of the following conditions, the eligible entity is deemed to meet that condition:

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: either of the conditions in paragraph (d) of the definition qualifying entity in subsection (1), and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: both of the conditions in subparagraph (c)(ii), or the condition in subparagraph (c)(iii), of the definition qualifying renter in subsection (1); and

Subsection.Text: Paragraphs 125.7(5)(a) and (b) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the amount of any deemed overpayment by an eligible entity under any of subsections (2) to (2.2) in respect of a qualifying period cannot exceed the amount claimed by the eligible entity in the application referred to in paragraph (a) of the definition qualifying entity in subsection (1) — or paragraph (a) of the definition qualifying renter in subsection (1) or paragraph (a) of the definition qualifying recovery entity in subsection (1) — in respect of that qualifying period; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: if an eligible employee is employed in a week by two or more qualifying entities that do not deal with each other at arm’s length, the total amount of the deemed overpayment under subsection (2) or (2.2) in respect of the eligible employee for that week shall not exceed the amount that would arise if the eligible employee’s eligible remuneration for that week were paid by one qualifying entity.

Subsection.Text: Subparagraph 125.7(6)(b)(ii) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: in respect of the fifth qualifying period and subsequent qualifying periods, increase the amount of a deemed overpayment under subsection (2), or

Subsection.Text: Section 125.7 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (6):

Notwithstanding any other provision in this section, the total current period remuneration of an eligible entity for a qualifying period is deemed to be equal to the total base period remuneration of the eligible entity, if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the eligible entity, or a person or partnership not dealing at arm’s length with the eligible entity, enters into a transaction or participates in an event (or a series of transactions or events) or takes an action (or fails to take an action) that has the effect of increasing the difference between the total current period remuneration and the total base period remuneration of the eligible entity for the qualifying period; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: it is reasonable to conclude that one of the main purposes of the transaction, event, series or action in paragraph (a) is to increase the amount of a deemed overpayment under subsection (2.2).

Subsection.Text: Paragraphs 125.7(7)(a) and (b) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: for the purposes of subsections (2) to (2.2) and subsections 152(3.4) and 160.1(1), to be a taxpayer; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: for the purposes of subsections (2) to (2.2), to have a liability under this Part for a taxation year in which a qualifying period ends.

Subsection.Text: Subparagraphs 125.7(8)(a)(i) and (ii) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the percentages in subparagraphs (a)(i), (b)(i), (c)(i), (d)(i), (e)(i), (f)(i), (g)(i), (h)(i), (i)(i) and (j)(i), and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the factors in subparagraphs (a)(ii), (b)(ii), (c)(ii), (d)(ii), (e)(ii), (f)(ii), (g)(ii), (h)(ii), (i)(ii) and (j)(ii); and

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 125.7(8)(b) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the definition rent subsidy percentage in subsection (1), the factors and percentages in paragraphs (a) and (a.1) of that definition;

Paragraph.Label: (b.1)
Paragraph.Text: the definition recovery wage subsidy rate in subsection (1), the percentages in that definition; and

Subsection.Text: Section 125.7 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (9):

For the purposes of paragraph (9)(b), if the particular qualifying period is the eleventh qualifying period, then the immediately preceding qualifying period is deemed to be the ninth qualifying period.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Greater of wage and recovery subsidies
Subsection.Text: For a qualifying period,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: if the amount of any deemed overpayment under subsection (2) is equal to or greater than the amount of any deemed overpayment under subsection (2.2), the amount of any deemed overpayment under subsection (2.2) is deemed to be nil; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: if the amount of any deemed overpayment under subsection (2.2) is greater than the amount of any deemed overpayment under subsection (2), the amount of any deemed overpayment under subsection (2) is deemed to be nil.

Subsection.Text: Section 125.7 of the Act is amended by adding the following in numerical order:

The amount of a refund made by the Minister to an eligible entity in respect of a deemed overpayment under subsection (2) on a particular date under subsection 164(1.6), in respect of any of the seventeenth qualifying period to the twenty-second qualifying period, is deemed to be an amount that has been refunded to the eligible entity on that particular date (for the taxation year in which the refund was made) in excess of the amount to which the eligible entity was entitled as a refund under this Act to the extent of the lesser of the amount of the refund and the amount determined by the formula

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the executive compensation repayment amount of the eligible entity; and
is the total of all amounts deemed to be an excess refund to the eligible entity under this subsection in respect of refunds made after the particular date.

Subsection.Text: For the purposes of paragraphs 261(2)(b) and (5)(c), amounts referred to in the definition executive remuneration in subsection (1) are deemed to arise on the last day of the eligible entity’s fiscal period to which the amount relates and not at any other time.
The Minister of Finance must prepare a report on proposed measures to

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: prevent publicly traded companies and their subsidiaries from paying dividends or repurchasing their own shares while receiving the Canada Emergency Wage Subsidy, for the period that is after the tabling of the report under subsection (32.2); and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: recover wage subsidy amounts from publicly traded companies and their subsidiaries that paid dividends or repurchased their own shares while receiving the Canada Emergency Wage Subsidy, for the period that is before the tabling of the report under subsection (32.2).

Subsection.Text: The Minister of Finance must cause the report to be tabled in each House of Parliament no later than 30 days after the day on which this Act receives royal assent or, if either House is not then sitting, on any of the first 15 days on which that House is sitting.
Subsections (12) and (24) are deemed to have come into force on September 27, 2020.
Subclause 126(1)(b)(ii)(A)(III) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subclause.Label: (III)
Subclause.Text: the total of all amounts each of which is an amount deducted under section 110.6 or paragraph 111(1)(b), or deductible under any of paragraphs 110(1)(d) to (g) and sections 112 and 113, in computing the taxpayer’s taxable income for the year, and

Subsection.Text: Subclause 126(2.1)(a)(ii)(A)(III) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subclause.Label: (III)
Subclause.Text: the total of all amounts each of which is an amount deducted under section 110.6 or paragraph 111(1)(b), or deductible under any of paragraphs 110(1)(d) to (g) and sections 112 and 113, in computing the taxpayer’s taxable income for the year, and

Subsection.Text: Subsections (1) and (2) come into force or are deemed to have come into force on July 1, 2021.
The portion of paragraph 128.1(1)(c.3) of the Act before subparagraph (i) is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (c.3)
Paragraph.Text: if the taxpayer is a corporation that was, immediately before the particular time, controlled by one non-resident person or, if no single non-resident person controlled the CRIC, a group of non-resident persons not dealing with each other at arm’s length (in this section, that one non-resident person, or each member of the group of non-resident persons, as the case may be, is referred to as a “parent”, and the group of non-resident persons, if any, is referred to as the “group of parents”) and the taxpayer owned, immediately before the particular time, one or more shares of one or more non-resident corporations (each of which is in this paragraph referred to as a “subject affiliate”) that, immediately after the particular time, were — or that became, as part of a transaction or event or series of transactions or events that includes the taxpayer having become resident in Canada — foreign affiliates of the taxpayer, then

Subsection.Text: Subparagraph 128.1(1)(c.3)(ii) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: for the purposes of Part XIII, the taxpayer is deemed, immediately after the particular time, to have paid to each parent, and each parent is deemed, immediately after the particular time, to have received from the taxpayer, a dividend in an amount determined by the formula

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the amount determined under clause (B) of the description of A in subparagraph (i),
is the amount determined under clause (A) of the description of A in subparagraph (i),
is the fair market value, immediately after the particular time, of the shares of the capital stock of the taxpayer that are held, directly or indirectly, by the parent, and

is the total of all amounts each of which is the fair market value, immediately after the particular time, of the shares of the capital stock of the taxpayer that are held, directly or indirectly, by a parent.

Subsection.Text: Subsections (1) and (2) apply in respect of transactions or events that occur after March 18, 2019.
Section 132 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (5.2):

If a trust that is a mutual fund trust throughout a taxation year paid or made payable, at any time in the taxation year, to a beneficiary an amount on a redemption by that beneficiary of a unit of the trust (in this subsection referred to as the “allocated amount”), and the beneficiary’s proceeds from the disposition of that unit do not include the allocated amount, in computing its income for the taxation year no deduction may be made by the trust in respect of

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the portion of the allocated amount that would be, without reference to subsection 104(6), an amount paid out of the income (other than taxable capital gains) of the trust; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the portion of the allocated amount determined by the formula

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the portion of the allocated amount that would be, without reference to subsection 104(6), an amount paid out of the taxable capital gains of the trust,
is the beneficiary’s proceeds from the disposition of the unit on the redemption,
is the allocated amount, and
is the amount determined by the trustee to be the beneficiary’s cost amount of that unit, using reasonable efforts to obtain the information required to determine the cost amount.

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) applies to taxation years that begin after March 18, 2019. However, paragraph 132(5.3)(b) of the Act, as enacted by subsection (1), does not apply to a taxation year of a mutual fund trust that begins before December 16, 2021, if, in that taxation year, units of the trust are

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: listed on a designated stock exchange in Canada; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: in continuous distribution.

Section.Text: Paragraph (a) of the definition tax deferred cooperative share in subsection 135.1(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: issued, after 2005 and before 2026, by an agricultural cooperative corporation to a person or partnership that is at the time the share is issued an eligible member of the agricultural cooperative corporation, pursuant to an allocation in proportion to patronage;

Subsection.Text: Subsection 143.3(5) of the Act is amended by striking out “and” at the end of paragraph (c), by adding “and” at the end of paragraph (d) and by adding the following after paragraph (d):

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: this section does not apply to prohibit the deduction of an amount under paragraph 110(1)(e).

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) comes into force or is deemed to have come into force on July 1, 2021.
The definition designated employee benefit in subsection 144.1(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Definition.Text: designated employee benefit means a benefit that is

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: from a group sickness or accident insurance plan;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: from a group term life insurance policy;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: from a private health services plan;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: in respect of a counselling service described in subparagraph 6(1)(a)(iv); or

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: not a death benefit, but that would be a death benefit if the amounts determined for paragraphs (a) and (b) of the definition death benefit in subsection 248(1) were nil. (prestation désignée)

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 144.1(2)(a) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the only purpose of the trust is to provide benefits to, or for the benefit of, persons described in subparagraph (d)(i) or (ii) and all or substantially all of the total cost of the benefits is applicable to designated employee benefits;

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 144.1(2)(c) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the trust meets one of the following conditions:

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the trust is required to be resident in Canada, determined without reference to section 94, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: if the condition in subparagraph (i) is not met, it is the case that

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: employee benefits are provided to employees who are resident in Canada and to employees who are not resident in Canada,

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: one or more participating employers are employers that are resident in a country other than Canada, and

Clause.Label: (C)
Clause.Text: the trust is required to be resident in a country in which a participating employer resides;

Subsection.Text: Subparagraph 144.1(2)(d)(i) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: an employee of a participating employer or former participating employer,

Subsection.Text: The portion of subparagraph 144.1(2)(d)(ii) of the Act before clause (A) is replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: an individual who, in respect of an employee of a participating employer or former participating employer, is (or, if the employee is deceased, was, at the time of the employee’s death)

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 144.1(2)(e) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: the trust meets one of the following conditions:

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: it contains at least one class of beneficiaries where

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: the members of the class represent at least 25% of all of the beneficiaries of the trust who are employees of the participating employers under the trust, and

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: either of the following conditions is met:

Subclause.Label: (I)
Subclause.Text: at least 75% of the members of the class are not key employees of any of the participating employers under the trust, or

Subclause.Label: (II)
Subclause.Text: the contributions to the trust in respect of key employees who deal at arm’s length with their employer are determined in connection with a collective bargaining agreement, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: in respect of the private health services plan under the trust, the total cost of benefits provided to each key employee (and to persons described in subparagraph (2)(d)(ii) in respect of the key employee) in relation to the year does not exceed the amount determined by the formula

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the total number of persons each of whom

FormulaParagraph.Label: (A)
FormulaParagraph.Text: is a person to whom designated employee benefits are provided under the plan, and

FormulaParagraph.Label: (B)
FormulaParagraph.Text: is the key employee or a person described in subparagraph (2)(d)(ii) in respect of the key employee,

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the number of days in the year that the key employee was employed on a full-time basis by an employer that participates in the plan, and
is the number of days in the year;

Subsection.Text: Subsection 144.1(2) of the Act is amended by adding “and” at the end of paragraph (g) and by replacing paragraphs (h) and (i) with the following:

Paragraph.Label: (i)
Paragraph.Text: trustees who do not deal at arm’s length with one or more participating employers must not constitute the majority of the trustees of the trust.

Subsection.Text: Paragraphs 144.1(3)(a) and (b) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: is not operated in accordance with the terms required by subsection (2) to govern the trust, unless it is reasonable to conclude that its trustees neither knew nor ought to have known that designated employee benefits have been provided to, or contributions have been made in respect of, beneficiaries other than those described in subparagraph (2)(d)(i) or (ii); or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: provides any benefit for which, if the benefit had been paid directly to the employee and not out of the trust, the contributions or premiums would not be deductible in computing the income of an employer in respect of any taxation year.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 144.1(6) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.MarginalNote: Deductibility — collectively bargained or similar agreement
Subsection.Text: Despite subsection (4) and paragraph 18(9)(a), an employer may deduct in computing its income for a taxation year the amount that it is required to contribute for the year to an employee life and health trust if the following conditions are met at the time that the contribution is made:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the employer contributes to the trust in accordance with a contribution formula that does not provide for any variation in contributions determined by reference to the financial experience of the trust and either of the following conditions is met:

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: if there is a collective bargaining agreement, the trust provides benefits

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: negotiated under the collective bargaining agreement, or

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: under a participation agreement that are substantially the same as under the collective bargaining agreement, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: in any other case, the trust provides benefits in accordance with an arrangement that meets the following conditions:

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: there is a legal requirement for each employer to participate in accordance with the terms and conditions that govern the trust,

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: there are a minimum of 50 beneficiaries under the trust who are employees of the participating employers in respect of the trust, and

Clause.Label: (C)
Clause.Text: each employee who is a beneficiary under the trust deals at arm’s length with each participating employer in respect of the trust; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: contributions that are to be made by each employer are determined, in whole or in part, by reference to the number of hours worked by individual employees of the employer or some other measure that is specific to each employee with respect to whom contributions are made to the trust.

Subsection.Text: Section 144.1 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (13):

Subsection.MarginalNote: Conditions — deemed employee life and health trust
Subsection.Text: Subsection (15) applies in respect of a trust if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the trust was established before February 28, 2018;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the contributions to the trust are determined in connection with a collective bargaining agreement;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: all or substantially all of the employee benefits provided by the trust are designated employee benefits; and

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: the trust elects in prescribed form and manner that subsection (15) applies as of a particular date after 2018.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Deemed employee life and health trust
Subsection.Text: If this subsection applies in respect of a trust,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the trust is deemed for the purposes of the Act to be an employee life and health trust from the particular date referred to in paragraph (14)(d) until the earliest of

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the end of 2022,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the day that the trust satisfies the conditions in subsection (2), and

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: any day on which the condition in paragraph (14)(c) is not satisfied; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: at any time that the trust is an employee life and health trust because of paragraph (a),

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: subsection 111(7.5) applies to the trust as if the reference in paragraph (b) of that subsection to “subsection 144.1(3)” were read as a reference to “paragraph 144.1(3)(b)”, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: subsection (3) applies to the trust without reference to its paragraph (a).

Subsection.Text: If a property is transferred from a trust that provides employee benefits substantially all of which are designated employee benefits (referred to in this subsection as the “transferor trust”) to an employee life and health trust (referred to in this subsection as the “receiving trust”), and if the Minister has been so notified in prescribed form, then

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the transferred property is deemed to have been disposed of by the transferor trust, and to have been acquired by the receiving trust, for an amount equal to the cost amount of the property to the transferor trust immediately before the disposition; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: section 107.1 does not apply to the transfer.

Subsection.Text: If subsection (16) applies to a transfer of property to an employee life and health trust, the transfer shall not be considered to be a contribution to the employee life and health trust for the purposes of subsections (4) and (6).
A trust shall, on or before its first filing-due date after 2021, notify the Minister in prescribed form that it is an employee life and health trust if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: prior to February 27, 2018, it provided employee benefits substantially all of which are designated employee benefits;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: after February 26, 2018, it becomes an employee life and health trust because it satisfies the conditions in subsection (2); and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: subsections (15) and (16) do not apply to the trust.

Subsection.Text: Subsections (1) to (10) are deemed to have come into force on February 27, 2018. As of that date, section 144.1 of the Act, as amended by subsections (1) to (10), applies in respect of trusts regardless of the date that the trust was established.
The description of A in subsection 146(1.1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the amount determined for F in subsection 118(1.1) for that preceding taxation year; and

Subsection.Text: Subsection 146(16) of the Act is amended by striking out “or” at the end of paragraph (a) and by adding the following after that paragraph:

Paragraph.Label: (a.1)
Paragraph.Text: to a licensed annuities provider to acquire an advanced life deferred annuity for the benefit of the transferor, or

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) applies to the 2021 and subsequent taxation years.
Subsection (2) is deemed to have come into force on January 1, 2020.

Paragraph 146.3(2)(f) of the Act is amended by striking out “or” at the end of subparagraph (vii), by adding “or” at the end of subparagraph (viii) and by adding the following after subparagraph (viii):

Subparagraph.Label: (ix)
Subparagraph.Text: an advanced life deferred annuity under which the individual is the annuitant, if the transfer is a refund described under paragraph (g) of the definition advanced life deferred annuity in subsection 146.5(1);

Subsection.Text: Subsection 146.3(14.1) of the Act is amended by striking out “or” at the end of paragraph (a), by adding “or” at the end of paragraph (b) and by adding the following after paragraph (b):

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: is transferred at the direction of the annuitant directly to a licensed annuities provider to acquire an advanced life deferred annuity for the benefit of the annuitant.

Subsection.Text: Subsections (1) and (2) are deemed to have come into force on January 1, 2020.
Paragraph (c) of the definition disability savings plan in subsection 146.4(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: that is entered into in a taxation year in respect of which

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the beneficiary is a DTC-eligible individual, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the beneficiary is not a DTC-eligible individual and an amount is to be transferred from a registered disability savings plan of the beneficiary to the arrangement in accordance with subsection (8). (régime d’épargne-invalidité)

Subsection.Text: Subparagraph 146.4(4)(f)(i) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the beneficiary is not a DTC-eligible individual in respect of the taxation year that includes that time, unless the contribution is a specified RDSP payment in respect of the beneficiary, or

Subsection.Text: The portion of subparagraph 146.4(4)(n)(i) of the Act before clause (A) is replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: if the calendar year is not a specified year for the plan and the conditions in clauses (p)(ii)(A) and (B) are not met in the calendar year, the total amount of disability assistance payments made from the plan to the beneficiary in the calendar year shall not exceed the specified maximum amount for the calendar year, except that, in calculating that total amount, any payment made following a transfer in the calendar year from another plan in accordance with subsection (8) is to be disregarded if it is made

Subsection.Text: Subparagraph 146.4(4)(p)(ii) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the first calendar year in which the following conditions are met:

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: the holder of the plan has requested that the issuer terminate the plan, and

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: throughout the year, the beneficiary has no severe and prolonged impairments with the effects described in paragraph 118.3(1)(a.1).

Subsection.Text: Section 146.4 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (4):

If, after March 18, 2019 and before 2021, a registered disability savings plan would otherwise be required to be terminated because of subparagraph (4)(p)(ii) or any terms of the plan provided because of that subparagraph, then notwithstanding that subparagraph or those terms, the plan is not required to be terminated before 2021 in either of the following circumstances:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the beneficiary of the plan has no severe and prolonged impairments with the effects described in paragraph 118.3(1)(a.1), or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: an election was made under subsection (4.1), as it read immediately before 2021, and the election ceases to be valid after March 18, 2019 and before 2021 because of paragraph (4.2)(b), as it read immediately before 2021.

Subsection.Text: Subsections 146.4(4.1) to (4.3) of the Act are repealed.
Subsections (1) to (4) and (6) are deemed to have come into force on January 1, 2021.
The Act is amended by adding the following after section 146.4:
The following definitions apply in this section.

Definition.Text: advanced life deferred annuity means a contract for an annuity that meets the following conditions:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: it is issued by a licensed annuities provider;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: it specifies that it is intended to qualify as an advanced life deferred annuity under this Act;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: periodic annuity payments under the contract

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: commence to be paid no later than the end of the calendar year in which the annuitant attains 85 years of age, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: are payable for the life of the annuitant or for the lives, jointly, of the annuitant and the annuitant’s spouse or common-law partner;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: periodic annuity payments under the contract are payable

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: in equal amounts, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: in amounts that are not equal only because the payments

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: are adjusted in whole or in part to reflect

Subclause.Label: (I)
Subclause.Text: increases in the Consumer Price Index, as published by Statistics Canada under the authority of the Statistics Act, or

Subclause.Label: (II)
Subclause.Text: increases at a rate specified in the contract, not exceeding 2% per annum, or

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: are reduced on the death of the annuitant or the annuitant’s spouse or common-law partner;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: if an annuity is payable for the lives, jointly, of the annuitant and the annuitant’s spouse or common-law partner and the annuitant dies before payments commence to be paid, then the payments to the annuitant’s spouse or common-law partner shall

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: commence no later than the date that they would have commenced if the annuitant were alive, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: be adjusted in accordance with generally accepted actuarial principles if the payments commence before the date they would have commenced if the annuitant were alive;

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: the amount to be paid, if any, to one or more beneficiaries under the contract after the death of the annuitant — or, in the case of a joint-lives annuity, after the last death of the annuitant and the annuitant’s spouse or common-law partner — shall

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: be paid as soon as practicable after the death of the annuitant or the last death of the annuitant and the annuitant’s spouse or common-law partner, as the case may be, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: not exceed the amount, if any, by which the total amount transferred to acquire the annuity exceeds the total amount of annuity payments made under the contract;

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: it provides that all or part of the amount transferred to acquire the annuity may be refunded, if

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the refund is paid to reduce the amount of tax that would otherwise be payable by the annuitant under Part XI, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the refund is

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: paid to the annuitant, or

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: transferred directly to

Subclause.Label: (I)
Subclause.Text: the issuer of a registered retirement savings plan of the annuitant,

Subclause.Label: (II)
Subclause.Text: the carrier of a registered retirement income fund of the annuitant,

Subclause.Label: (III)
Subclause.Text: the administrator of a pooled registered pension plan under which the annuitant is a member, or

Subclause.Label: (IV)
Subclause.Text: the administrator of a money purchase provision of a registered pension plan under which the annuitant is a member;

Paragraph.Label: (h)
Paragraph.Text: if it provides that the spouse or common-law partner may request a payment in a single amount in full or partial satisfaction of the spouse’s or common-law partner’s entitlement to payments described in subparagraph (c)(ii) as a consequence of the death of the annuitant, then the single amount cannot exceed the present value (at the time the single amount is paid) of the other payments that, as a consequence of the payment of the single amount, cease to be provided;

Paragraph.Label: (i)
Paragraph.Text: no right under the contract is capable of being assigned, charged, anticipated, given as security or surrendered; and

Paragraph.Label: (j)
Paragraph.Text: it does not provide for any payment under the contract except as specified in this definition. (rente viagère différée à un âge avancé)

Definition.Text: annuitant means an individual who has acquired a contract for an annuity from a licensed annuities provider. (rentier)

beneficiary, under a contract for an annuity, means an individual who has a right under the contract to receive a payment after the death of the annuitant or the annuitant’s spouse or common-law partner. (bénéficiaire)

Subsection.Text: Amounts (excluding amounts described in paragraph (f) or (g) of the definition advanced life deferred annuity in subsection (1) and including amounts deemed to have been received under paragraph (7)(a)) received by a taxpayer in a taxation year under an advanced life deferred annuity shall be included in computing the income of the taxpayer for the taxation year.

Amounts described in paragraph (f) of the definition advanced life deferred annuity in subsection (1) received by a taxpayer in a taxation year under an advanced life deferred annuity as a result of the death of an individual shall be included in computing the income of

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the taxpayer for the taxation year, if the taxpayer is

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the spouse or common-law partner of the individual, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: a child or grandchild of the individual who was, immediately before the death of the individual, financially dependent on the individual for support; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the individual for the taxation year in which the individual died, in any other case.

Subsection.Text: The amount of a refund described in clause (g)(ii)(A) of the definition advanced life deferred annuity in subsection (1) that is paid to an annuitant shall be included in the income of the annuitant.
If an amount is paid in circumstances described in clause (g)(ii)(B) of the definition advanced life deferred annuity in subsection (1),

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the amount shall not, by reason only of that payment, be included by reason of paragraph 56(1)(z.5) in computing the income of any taxpayer;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: no deduction may be made under any provision of this Act in respect of the amount in computing the income of any taxpayer;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of an amount paid to a registered pension plan, the amount is deemed not to be a contribution for the purpose of applying Parts LXXXIII and LXXXV of the Income Tax Regulations; and

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of an amount paid to a registered retirement savings plan or a pooled registered pension plan, the amount shall not be included in determining the amount of the individual’s undeducted RRSP premiums under subsection 204.2(1.2).

Subsection.Text: An amount is deemed to have been received at a particular time by a beneficiary (as defined in subsection 108(1)) of a deceased annuitant’s estate, and not by the legal representative of the deceased annuitant, if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the amount is described in paragraph (f) of the definition advanced life deferred annuity in subsection (1);

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the amount was paid to the legal representative;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the beneficiary is described in paragraph (3)(a);

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: the beneficiary is entitled to the amount in full or partial satisfaction of their rights as a beneficiary under the deceased annuitant’s estate; and

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: the amount is designated jointly by the legal representative and the beneficiary in prescribed form filed with the Minister.

Subsection.Text: If an amendment made at any time to a contract results in it no longer meeting the conditions in the definition advanced life deferred annuity in subsection (1), the following rules apply:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the annuitant under the contract immediately before that time is deemed to have received under the contract at that time an amount equal to the fair market value of their interest in the contract at that time; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the annuitant is deemed to have acquired their interest in the contract at that time at a cost equal to the fair market value of the interest at that time.

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) is deemed to have come into force on January 1, 2020.
The portion of subparagraph 147(2)(k)(vi) of the Act before clause (A) is replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (vi)
Subparagraph.Text: by a trustee under the plan to a licensed annuities provider to purchase for the beneficiary an annuity (other than an advanced life deferred annuity), if

Subsection.Text: Subparagraph 147(2)(k.1)(ii.1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (ii.1)
Subparagraph.Text: an amount paid pursuant to or under the plan by a trustee under the plan to a licensed annuities provider to purchase for a beneficiary under the plan an annuity to which subparagraph (k)(vi) or (19)(d)(v) applies,

Subsection.Text: The portion of paragraph 147(19)(d) of the French version of the Act before subparagraph (i) is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: d)
Paragraph.Text: le montant est transféré directement à l’un des régimes, fonds ou fournisseurs ci-après au profit du particulier :

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 147(19)(d) of the Act is amended by striking out “or” at the end of subparagraph (iii), by adding “or” at the end of subparagraph (iv) and by adding the following after subparagraph (iv):

Subparagraph.Label: (v)
Subparagraph.Text: a licensed annuities provider to acquire an advanced life deferred annuity, if the individual is an employee or former employee of an employer who participated in the plan on the employee’s behalf.

Paragraph.Label: c)

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 147.3(1)(c) of the Act is amended by striking out “or” at the end of subparagraph (ii), by adding “or” at the end of subparagraph (iii) and by adding the following after subparagraph (iii):

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: a licensed annuities provider to acquire an advanced life deferred annuity for the benefit of the member.

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 147.3(3)(c) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: is transferred directly to another registered pension plan to be held in connection with a defined benefit provision of the other plan, unless the transfer is to an individual pension plan (as defined in subsection 8300(1) of the Income Tax Regulations) and is in respect of benefits that are attributable to employment with a former employer that is not a participating employer (or its predecessor employer); and

Subsection.Text: Subsections (1) and (2) are deemed to have come into force on January 1, 2020.
Subsection (3) is deemed to have come into force on March 19, 2019.
Paragraph 147.4(1)(a) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: at any time an individual acquires, in full or partial satisfaction of the individual’s entitlement to benefits under a registered pension plan, an interest in an annuity contract (other than an advanced life deferred annuity) purchased from a licensed annuities provider,

The portion of the definition qualifying annuity in subsection 147.5(1) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

Definition.Text: qualifying annuity, for an individual, means an annuity (other than an advanced life deferred annuity) that

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 147.5(5)(a) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 147.5(21)(c) of the Act is amended by striking out “or” at the end of subparagraph (iv), by adding “or” at the end of subparagraph (v) and by adding the following after subparagraph (v):

Subparagraph.Label: (vi)
Subparagraph.Text: a licensed annuities provider to acquire an advanced life deferred annuity for the benefit of the member.

Subsection.Text: Subsections (1) to (3) are deemed to have come into force on January 1, 2020.
The definition ineligible individual in subsection 149.1(1) of the Act is amended by striking out “or” at the end of paragraph (d) and by adding the following after paragraph (e):

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: a listed terrorist entity, or a member of a listed terrorist entity,

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: a director, trustee, officer or like official of a listed terrorist entity during a period in which that entity supported or engaged in terrorist activities, including a period prior to the date on which the entity became a listed terrorist entity, or

Paragraph.Label: (h)
Paragraph.Text: an individual who controlled or managed, directly or indirectly, in any manner whatever, a listed terrorist entity during a period in which that entity supported or engaged in terrorist activities, including a period prior to the date on which the entity became a listed terrorist entity; (particulier non admissable)

Subsection.Text: The definition qualifying journalism organization in subsection 149.1(1) of the Act is amended by striking out “and” at the end of paragraph (f), by adding “and” at the end of paragraph (g) and by adding the following after paragraph (g):

Paragraph.Label: (h)
Paragraph.Text: it is primarily engaged in the production of original news content; (organisation journalistique admissible)

Subsection.Text: Subsection 149.1(1) of the Act is amended by adding the following in alphabetical order:

Definition.Text: listed terrorist entity, at any time, means a person, partnership, group, fund, unincorporated association or organization that is at that time a listed entity, as defined in subsection 83.01(1) of the Criminal Code; (entité terroriste inscrite)

Subsection.Text: Section 149.1 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (1.01):

If, but for this subsection, a person, partnership, group, fund, unincorporated association or organization becomes a listed terrorist entity at a particular time and ceases to be a listed terrorist entity at a later time further to an application made under subsection 83.05(2) of the Criminal Code or as a result of paragraph 83.05(6)(d) of that Act, then the entity is deemed not to have become a listed terrorist entity and to not have been a listed terrorist entity throughout that period.
Paragraph 149.1(4.1)(c) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: of a registered charity, if a false statement (as defined in subsection 163.2(1)) was made in circumstances amounting to culpable conduct (as defined in subsection 163.2(1)) in the furnishing of information for the purpose of obtaining or maintaining its registration;

Subsection.Text: Subsection (2) is deemed to have come into force on January 1, 2019.
Paragraph 152(1)(b) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the amount of tax, if any, deemed by subsection 120(2) or (2.2), 122.5(3), 122.51(2), 122.7(2) or (3), 122.8(4), 122.9(2), 122.91(1), 125.4(3), 125.5(3), 125.6(2) or (2.1), 127.1(1), 127.41(3) or 210.2(3) or (4) to be paid on account of the taxpayer’s tax payable under this Part for the year.

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 152(1)(b) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the amount of tax, if any, deemed by subsection 120(2) or (2.2), 122.5(3) or (3.001), 122.51(2), 122.7(2) or (3), 122.8(4), 122.9(2), 122.91(1), 125.4(3), 125.5(3), 125.6(2) or (2.1), 127.1(1), 127.41(3) or 210.2(3) or (4) to be paid on account of the taxpayer’s tax payable under this Part for the year.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 152(3.4) of the Act is replaced by the following:

The Minister may at any time determine the amount deemed by any of subsections 125.7(2) to (2.2) to be an overpayment on account of a taxpayer’s liability under this Part that arose during a qualifying period (as defined in subsection 125.7(1)), or determine that there is no such amount, and send a notice of the determination to the taxpayer.
Clause 152(4)(b)(iii)(A) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: as a consequence of a transaction (as defined in subsection 247(1)) involving the taxpayer and a non-resident person with whom the taxpayer was not dealing at arm’s length, or

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) is deemed to have come into force on January 1, 2019.
Subsection (2) is deemed to have come into force on March 25, 2020.

Subsection (4) applies to taxation years of a taxpayer in respect of which the normal reassessment period (within the meaning of subsection 152(3.1) of the Act) for the taxpayer ends after March 18, 2019.
Subsection 153(1) of the Act is amended by striking out “or” at the end of paragraph (s), by adding “or” at the end of paragraph (t) and by adding the following after paragraph (t):

Paragraph.Label: (u)
Paragraph.Text: a payment out of or under an advanced life deferred annuity

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) is deemed to have come into force on January 1, 2020.
Paragraph 157(3)(e) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: 1/12 of the total of the amounts each of which is deemed by subsection 125.4(3), 125.5(3), 125.6(2) or (2.1), 127.1(1) or 127.41(3) to have been paid on account of the corporation’s tax payable under this Part for the year.

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 157(3.1)(c) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: 1/4 of the total of the amounts each of which is deemed by subsection 125.4(3), 125.5(3), 125.6(2) or (2.1), 127.1(1) or 127.41(3) to have been paid on account of the corporation’s tax payable under this Part for the taxation year.

Subsection.Text: Subsections (1) and (2) are deemed to have come into force on January 1, 2019.
Subparagraph 163(2)(h)(i) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the amount that would be deemed by subsection 125.6(2) or (2.1) to have been paid for the year by the person if that amount were calculated by reference to the information provided in the return filed for the year pursuant to that subsection

Subsection.Text: Subparagraph 163(2)(i)(i) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the amount that would be deemed by any of subsections 125.7(2) to (2.2) to have been an overpayment by the person or partnership if that amount were calculated by reference to the information provided in the application filed pursuant to paragraph (a) of the definition qualifying entity in subsection 125.7(1), paragraph (a) of the definition qualifying renter in subsection 125.7(1) or paragraph (a) of the definition qualifying recovery entity in subsection 125.7(1), as the case may be

Subsection.Text: Section 163 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (2.901):

Every eligible entity that is deemed by subsection 125.7(6.1) to have an amount of total current period remuneration for a qualifying period is liable to a penalty equal to 25% of the amount that would be deemed by subsection 125.7(2.2) to have been an overpayment by the eligible entity during that qualifying period if that amount were calculated by reference to the information provided in the application filed pursuant to paragraph (a) of the definition qualifying recovery entity in subsection 125.7(1).
Subsection (1) is deemed to have come into force on January 1, 2019.
Subparagraph 164(1)(a)(ii) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: before sending the notice of assessment for the year, where the taxpayer is a qualified corporation (as defined in subsection 125.4(1)), an eligible production corporation (as defined in subsection 125.5(1)) or a qualifying journalism organization (as defined in subsection 125.6(1)) and an amount is deemed under subsection 125.4(3), 125.5(3) or 125.6(2) or (2.1) to have been paid on account of its tax payable under this Part for the year, refund all or part of any amount claimed in the return as an overpayment for the year, not exceeding the total of those amounts so deemed to have been paid, and

Subsection.Text: Subsection 164(1.6) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Notwithstanding subsection (2.01), at any time after the beginning of a taxation year of a taxpayer in which an overpayment is deemed to have arisen under any of subsections 125.7(2) to (2.2), the Minister may refund to the taxpayer all or any part of the overpayment.
Subsection (1) is deemed to have come into force on January 1, 2019.

Section.Text: Section 168 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (3):

Subsection.Text: Notwithstanding subsections (1), (2) and (4), if a qualified donee is a listed terrorist entity for the purposes of section 149.1, the registration of the qualified donee is revoked as of the date on which it became a listed terrorist entity.
The Act is amended by adding the following after section 168:

Heading.TitleText: Designation of Qualified Canadian Journalism Organizations

Subsection.Text: If an organization is designated for the purpose of the definition qualified Canadian journalism organization in subsection 248(1), the organization is deemed to have become designated on the date that the application for designation of the organization was made, unless otherwise specified by the Minister.

The Minister may, at any time, revoke the designation of an organization made for the purpose of the definition qualified Canadian journalism organization in subsection 248(1) and, for that purpose, the Minister shall take into account any recommendations of a body established for the purpose of that definition and referred to in paragraph (b) of that definition.
If the designation of an organization is revoked under subsection (2),

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the Minister shall provide notice of the revocation to the organization in writing; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the revocation is deemed to be effective as of the date on which the notice in paragraph (a) is sent, unless the Minister specifies an earlier date.

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) is deemed to have come into force on January 1, 2019.

Section.Text: The portion of subsection 188(1) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Deemed year-end on notice of revocation

Subsection.Text: If on a particular day the Minister issues a notice of intention to revoke the registration of a taxpayer as a registered charity under any of subsections 149.1(2) to (4.1) and 168(1), it becomes a listed terrorist entity or it is determined, under subsection 7(1) of the Charities Registration (Security Information) Act, that a certificate served in respect of the charity under subsection 5(1) of that Act is reasonable on the basis of information and evidence available,

Section.Text: Subsection 188.2(2) of the Act is amended by striking out “or” at the end of paragraph (d), by adding “or” at the end of paragraph (e) and by adding the following after paragraph (e):

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of a person that is a registered charity, if a false statement (as defined in subsection 163.2(1)) was made in circumstances amounting to culpable conduct (as defined in subsection 163.2(1)) in the furnishing of information for the purpose of maintaining its registration.

Subsection.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after Part X.5:

Heading.Label: PART XI
Heading.TitleText: Tax in Respect of Advanced Life Deferred Annuity

Subsection.Text: The following definitions apply in this section.

Definition.Text: ALDA dollar limit, for a calendar year, means

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: for each year after 2020, the amount (rounded to the nearest multiple of $10,000, or if that amount is equidistant from two such consecutive multiples, to the higher multiple) that is equal to $150,000 adjusted for each year after 2020 in the manner set out in section 117.1. (plafond de la RVDAA)

Definition.Text: cumulative excess amount, of an individual at any particular time in a calendar year, means the amount determined by the formula

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the greater of

FormulaParagraph.Label: (a)
FormulaParagraph.Text: the total of all amounts each of which is an excess ALDA transfer of the individual at or before the particular time, and

FormulaParagraph.Label: (b)
FormulaParagraph.Text: the amount determined by the formula

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the total of all amounts each of which is the amount of a transfer at or before the particular time to acquire an advanced life deferred annuity on behalf of the individual, and
is the ALDA dollar limit for the calendar year; and

is the total of all amounts each of which is the amount of a refund described in paragraph (g) of the definition advanced life deferred annuity in subsection 146.5(1) made at or before the particular time on behalf of the individual. (excédent cumulatif)

Definition.Text: excess ALDA transfer, of an individual, means the portion of the amount of a transfer, made from a transferor plan under any of subsections 146(16) and 146.3(14.1) and paragraphs 147(19)(d), 147.3(1)(c) and 147.5(21)(c) to acquire an advanced life deferred annuity on behalf of the individual, determined by the formula

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the amount of the transfer; and
is the amount determined by the formula

is the total value of the property held for the benefit of the individual under the transferor plan at the end of the calendar year preceding the calendar year in which the transfer is made, other than

FormulaParagraph.Label: (a)
FormulaParagraph.Text: if the transferor plan is a registered pension plan, property held in connection with

FormulaParagraph.Label: (i)
FormulaParagraph.Text: a defined benefit provision (as defined in subsection 147.1(1)) of the transferor plan, or

FormulaParagraph.Label: (ii)
FormulaParagraph.Text: a VPLA fund, as described in subsection 8506(13) of the Income Tax Regulations,

FormulaParagraph.Label: (b)
FormulaParagraph.Text: if the transferor plan is a pooled registered pension plan, property held in connection with benefits that would be described in paragraph 147.5(5)(a) if the reference in that paragraph to “8506(1)(e.1) or (e.2)” were read as a reference to “8506(1)(e.2)”,

FormulaParagraph.Label: (c)
FormulaParagraph.Text: if the transferor plan is a registered retirement income fund, contracts for annuities held in connection with the fund other than annuities described in paragraph (b.1) of the definition qualified investment in subsection 146.3(1), and

FormulaParagraph.Label: (d)
FormulaParagraph.Text: if the transferor plan is a registered retirement savings plan, contracts for annuities held in connection with the plan other than annuities described in paragraph (c.1) of the definition qualified investment in subsection 146(1),

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the total of all amounts each of which is the amount transferred from the transferor plan, in a calendar year preceding the calendar year in which the transfer is made, to acquire an advanced life deferred annuity on behalf of the individual, and

is the total of all amounts each of which is the amount of a previous transfer from the transferor plan to acquire an advanced life deferred annuity on behalf of the individual. (excédent de transfert au titre de la RVDAA)

Subsection.Text: If at the end of any month an individual has a cumulative excess amount, the individual shall, in respect of that month, pay a tax under this Part equal to 1% of that cumulative excess amount.

If an individual would, but for this subsection, be required to pay a tax under subsection (2) in respect of a month, the Minister may waive or cancel all or part of the tax if the individual establishes to the satisfaction of the Minister that

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the cumulative excess amount on which the tax is based arose as a consequence of reasonable error; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: reasonable steps are being taken to eliminate the cumulative excess amount.

Subsection.Text: Every person who is liable to pay tax under this Part for all or part of a calendar year shall,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: on or before the person’s filing-due date for the year, file with the Minister a return for the year under this Part in prescribed form and containing prescribed information, without notice or demand; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: on or before the person’s balance-due day for the year, pay to the Receiver General the amount of tax payable under this Part by the person for the year.

Subsection.Text: Subsections 150(2) and (3), sections 152 and 158 to 167 and Division J of Part I apply with any modifications that the circumstances require.
Subsection (1) is deemed to have come into force on January 1, 2020.
The Act is amended by adding the following after Part XI.4:

Heading.Label: PART XI.5
Heading.TitleText: Tax in Respect of Employee Life and Health Trust

Subsection.Text: The following definitions apply in this Part.

Definition.Text: participating employer of an employee life and health trust means an employer that provides designated employee benefits for its employees through a trust that meets the conditions described in subsection 144.1(2). (employeur participant)
prohibited investment, at any time for an employee life and health trust, means property that is at that time

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a share of the capital stock of, an interest in or a debt of

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: a participating employer of the employee life and health trust, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: a person or partnership that does not deal at arm’s length with a participating employer of the employee life and health trust; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: an interest (or, for civil law, a right) in, or a right to acquire, a share, interest or debt described in paragraph (a). (placement interdit)

Subsection.Text: A trust shall pay a tax under this Part for a calendar year if, at any time in the year while the trust is an employee life and health trust,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the trust acquires property that is a prohibited investment for the trust; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: income is received or becomes receivable by the trust from, or the trust has a taxable capital gain from the disposition of, a prohibited investment for the trust.

Subsection.Text: The amount of tax payable under subsection (2) is

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: if paragraph (2)(a) applies, 50% of the fair market value of the property at the time it is acquired; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: if paragraph (2)(b) applies, 50% of the income or the taxable capital gain.

Subsection.Text: If in a calendar year a trust disposes of a property in respect of which a tax is imposed on the trust under subsection (2), the trust is entitled to a refund for the year of an amount equal to

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the amount of the tax so imposed, unless paragraph (b) applies; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: nil, if

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: it is reasonable to consider that the trustees knew, or ought to have known, at the time the property was acquired that it was, or would become, a property described in subsection (2), or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the property is not disposed of by the trust before the end of the calendar year following the calendar year in which the tax arose, or any later time that the Minister considers reasonable in the circumstances.

Subsection.Text: If, at any time, a property held by an employee life and health trust ceases to be, or becomes, a prohibited investment for the employee life and health trust, the employee life and health trust is deemed to have disposed of the property immediately before that time for proceeds of disposition equal to the fair market value of the property at that time and to have reacquired the property at that time at a cost equal to that fair market value.
Subsection (1) applies to the 2014 and subsequent taxation years.

Section.Text: Section 211.91 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (2):

Subsection.Text: If an agreement referred to in subsection 66(12.66) was made in 2019 or 2020,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the reference in subsection (2) to “the following calendar year” is to be read as a reference to “the second following calendar year”; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: for the purposes of this section and, where subparagraph (iii) applies, paragraph 66(12.66)(a), Canadian exploration expenses incurred by a corporation in respect of the agreement in a particular month in a calendar year are deemed to have been incurred

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: in January 2020, if the expenses were incurred in 2020 and the agreement was entered into in 2019,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: in January 2021, if the expenses were incurred in 2021 and the agreement was entered into in 2020, and

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: 12 months earlier, in any other case.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 212(1) of the Act is amended by adding the following after paragraph (l):

Paragraph.Label: (l.1)
Paragraph.Text: a payment of an amount described in paragraph 56(1)(z.5);

Subsection.Text: The portion of subsection 212(2.1) of the Act before paragraph (c) is replaced by the following:
Subsection (2) does not apply to an amount paid or credited, by a borrower, under a securities lending arrangement or a specified securities lending arrangement if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the amount is deemed by subparagraph 260(8)(a)(ii) to be a dividend;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: either

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the arrangement is a fully collateralized arrangement, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the borrower and the lender are dealing at arm’s length; and

Subsection.Text: Paragraph (d) of the definition fully exempt interest in subsection 212(3) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: an amount paid or payable or credited under a securities lending arrangement, or a specified securities lending arrangement, that is deemed by subparagraph 260(8)(a)(i) to be a payment made by a borrower to a lender of interest, if the arrangement is a fully collateralized arrangement, and

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the following conditions are met:

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: the arrangement was entered into by the borrower in the course of carrying on a business outside Canada, and

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: the security that is transferred or lent to the borrower under the arrangement is described in paragraph (b) of the definition qualified security in subsection 260(1) and issued by a non-resident issuer,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the security that is transferred or lent to the borrower under the arrangement is described in paragraph (c) of the definition qualified security in subsection 260(1), or

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: the security that is transferred or lent to the borrower under the arrangement is described in paragraph (a) or (b). (intérêts entièrement exonérés)

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) is deemed to have come into force on January 1, 2020.
Subsections (2) and (3) apply in respect of amounts paid or payable or credited after March 18, 2019.
The portion of paragraph 212.3(1)(b) of the Act before clause (i)(A) is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the CRIC or an other Canadian corporation is immediately after the investment time, or becomes after the investment time and as part of a transaction or event or series of transactions or events that includes the making of the investment, controlled by one non-resident person or, if no single non-resident person controls the CRIC, by a group of non-resident persons not dealing with each other at arm’s length (in this section, that one non-resident person, or each member of the group of non-resident persons, as the case may be, is referred to as a “parent”, and the group of non-resident persons, if any, is referred to as the “group of parents”), and any of the following conditions is satisfied:

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: if, at the investment time, a parent owned all shares of the capital stock of the CRIC and the other Canadian corporation, if applicable, that are owned — determined without reference to paragraph (25)(b) in the case of partnerships referred to in this subparagraph and as if all rights referred to in paragraph 251(5)(b), of the parent, each person that does not deal at arm’s length with the parent and all of those partnerships, were immediate and absolute and the parent and each of the other persons and partnerships had exercised those rights at the investment time — by the parent, persons that are not dealing at arm’s length with the parent and partnerships of which the parent or a person that is not dealing at arm’s length with the parent is a member (other than a limited partner within the meaning assigned by subsection 96(2.4)), the parent would own shares of the capital stock of the CRIC or the other Canadian corporation that

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 212.3(2)(a) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: for the purposes of this Part and subject to subsections (3) and (7), the CRIC is deemed to have paid to each parent, and each parent is deemed to have received from the CRIC, at the dividend time, a dividend in an amount determined by the formula

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the total of all amounts each of which is the portion of the fair market value at the investment time of any property (not including shares of the capital stock of the CRIC) transferred, any obligation assumed or incurred, or any benefit otherwise conferred, by the CRIC, or of any property transferred to the CRIC which transfer results in the reduction of an amount owing to the CRIC, that can reasonably be considered to relate to the investment,
is

FormulaParagraph.Label: (i)
FormulaParagraph.Text: if there is one parent, one, and

FormulaParagraph.Label: (ii)
FormulaParagraph.Text: if there is a group of parents, the fair market value at the dividend time of the shares of the capital stock of the CRIC that are held, directly or indirectly, by the parent, and

FormulaDefinition.Text: is

FormulaParagraph.Label: (i)
FormulaParagraph.Text: if there is one parent, one, and

FormulaParagraph.Label: (ii)
FormulaParagraph.Text: if there is a group of parents, the total of all amounts each of which is the fair market value at the dividend time of the shares of the capital stock of the CRIC that are held, directly or indirectly, by a parent; and

Subsection.Text: The portion of subsection 212.3(3) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:
