If a CRIC (or a CRIC and a corporation that is a qualifying substitute corporation in respect of the CRIC at the dividend time) and a parent (or a parent and another non-resident person that at the dividend time is related to the parent) jointly elect in writing under this subsection in respect of an investment, and the election is filed with the Minister on or before the filing-due date of the CRIC for its taxation year that includes the dividend time, then the dividend that would, in the absence of this subsection, be deemed under paragraph (2)(a) to have been paid by the CRIC to the parent and received by the parent from the CRIC is deemed to have instead been
Paragraph 212.3(3)(b) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: paid to, and received by, the parent or the other non-resident person, as agreed on in the election.

Subsection.Text: Paragraphs (a) and (b) of the definition cross-border class in subsection 212.3(4) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a parent, or a non-resident person that does not deal at arm’s length with a parent, owns at least one share of the class; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: no more than 30% of the issued and outstanding shares of the class are owned by one or more persons resident in Canada that do not deal at arm’s length with a parent. (catégorie transfrontalière)

Subsection.Text: The portion of the definition dividend time before subparagraph (b)(ii) in subsection 212.3(4) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Definition.Text: dividend time, in respect of an investment, means

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: if the CRIC is controlled by a parent or group of parents at the investment time, the investment time; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: in any other case, the earlier of

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the first time, after the investment time, at which the CRIC is controlled by a parent or group of parents, as the case may be, and

Subsection.Text: Paragraphs (a) to (c) of the definition qualifying substitute corporation in subsection 212.3(4) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: that is, at that time, controlled by

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: a parent,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: a group of parents, or

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: a non-resident person that does not deal at arm’s length with a parent;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: that has, at that time, an equity percentage (as defined in subsection 95(4)) in the CRIC; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: shares of the capital stock of which are, at that time, owned by a parent or another non-resident person with which the parent does not, at that time, deal at arm’s length. (société de substitution admissible)

Subsection.Text: The portion of subsection 212.3(5.1) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

In the case of an investment (in this subsection referred to as the “second investment”) in a subject corporation by a CRIC described in paragraph (10)(f), the amount determined for A in paragraph (2)(a) in respect of the second investment is to be reduced by the amount determined for A in paragraph (2)(a) in respect of a prior investment (in this subsection referred to as the “first investment”) in the subject corporation by another corporation resident in Canada if
Paragraphs 212.3(5.1)(b) and (c) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: immediately after the investment time in respect of the first investment, the other corporation is not controlled by

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: if there is one parent in respect of the CRIC, the parent, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: if there is a group of parents in respect of the CRIC, the group of parents; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the other corporation becomes, after the time that is immediately after the investment time in respect of the first investment and as part of a transaction or event or series of transactions or events that includes the making of the first investment, controlled by the parent or group of parents, as the case may be, because of the second investment.

Subsection.Text: The portion of paragraph 212.3(6)(a) of the Act before subparagraph (i) is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a particular corporation resident in Canada that does not deal at arm’s length with a parent

Subsection.Text: The portion of clause 212.3(6)(a)(ii)(B) of the Act before subclause (I) is replaced by the following:

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: the increase in paid-up capital in respect of the particular class can reasonably be considered to be connected to funding provided to the particular corporation or another corporation resident in Canada (other than the corporation that issued the particular class) by a parent or a non-resident person that does not deal at arm’s length with a parent, unless

Subsection.Text: The portion of subparagraph 212.3(7)(a)(i) of the Act before clause (A) is replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the amount determined, without reference to this subsection, for A in paragraph (2)(a), is reduced by the lesser of

Subsection.Text: The portion of paragraph 212.3(7)(b) of the Act before subparagraph (ii) is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: where the amount determined, without reference to this paragraph, for A in paragraph (2)(a) is equal to or greater than the total of all amounts each of which is an amount of paid-up capital immediately after the dividend time, determined without reference to this paragraph, of a cross-border class in respect of the investment, then

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the amount determined, without reference to this paragraph, for A in paragraph (2)(a) is reduced by the total referred to in this paragraph, and

Subsection.Text: Paragraphs 212.3(7)(c) and (d) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: where paragraph (b) does not apply and there is at least one cross-border class in respect of the investment,

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the amount determined, without reference to this paragraph, for A in paragraph (2)(a) is reduced to nil,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: in computing, at any time after the dividend time, the paid-up capital in respect of a particular cross-border class in respect of the investment, there is to be deducted the amount, if any, that when added to the total of all amounts that are deducted under this paragraph in computing the paid-up capital of other cross-border classes, results in the greatest total reduction because of this paragraph, immediately after the dividend time, of the paid-up capital in respect of shares of cross-border classes that are owned by a parent or another non-resident person with which a parent does not, at the dividend time, deal at arm’s length,

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: if the proportion of the shares of a particular class owned, in aggregate, by parents and non-resident persons that do not deal at arm’s length with parents is equal to the proportion so owned of one or more other cross-border classes (in this subparagraph all those classes, together with the particular class, referred to as the “relevant classes”), then the proportion that the reduction under subparagraph (ii) to the paid-up capital in respect of the particular class is of the paid-up capital, determined immediately after the dividend time and without reference to this paragraph, in respect of that class is to be equal to the proportion that the total reduction under subparagraph (ii) to the paid-up capital in respect of all the relevant classes is of the total paid-up capital, determined immediately after the dividend time and without reference to this paragraph, of all the relevant classes, and

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: the total of all amounts each of which is an amount to be deducted under subparagraph (ii) in computing the paid-up capital of a cross-border class is to be equal to the amount by which the amount determined for A in paragraph (2)(a) is reduced under subparagraph (i); and

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: if the amount determined for A in paragraph (2)(a) is reduced because of any of subparagraphs (a)(i), (b)(i) and (c)(i),

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the CRIC shall file with the Minister in prescribed manner a form containing prescribed information and the amounts of the paid-up capital, determined immediately after the dividend time and without reference to this subsection, of each class of shares that is described in paragraph (a) or that is a cross-border class in respect of the investment, the paid-up capital of the shares of each of those classes that are owned by a parent or another non-resident person that does not, at the dividend time, deal at arm’s length with a parent, and the reduction under any of subparagraphs (a)(ii), (b)(ii) and (c)(ii) in respect of each of those classes, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: if the form is not filed on or before the CRIC’s filing-due date for its taxation year that includes the dividend time, the CRIC is deemed to have paid to each parent, and each parent is deemed to have received from the CRIC, on the filing-due date, a dividend equal to the total of all amounts each of which is the amount of a reduction because of any of subparagraphs (a)(i), (b)(i) and (c)(i) in the amount the CRIC is deemed under paragraph (2)(a) to have paid to the parent.

Subsection.Text: The portion of paragraph 212.3(11)(c) of the Act before subparagraph (i) is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the CRIC and each parent jointly elect in writing under this paragraph in respect of the amount owing and file the election with the Minister on or before the filing-due date of the CRIC

Subsection.Text: Paragraphs 212.3(15)(a) and (b) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a CRIC or a taxpayer to which paragraph 128.1(1)(c.3) applies (in this subsection referred to as the “specific corporation”), that would, in the absence of this subsection, be controlled at any time

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: by more than one non-resident person, is deemed not to be controlled at that time by any such person that controls at that time another non-resident person that controls at that time the specific corporation, unless the application of this paragraph would otherwise result in no non-resident person controlling the specific corporation, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: by a particular non-resident corporation is deemed not to be controlled at that time by the particular non-resident corporation if the particular non-resident corporation is controlled at that time by another corporation that is at that time

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: resident in Canada, and

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: not controlled by any non-resident person or group of non-resident persons not dealing with each other at arm’s length; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a non-resident person is deemed not to be a member of a particular group of non-resident persons not dealing with each other at arm’s length that controls the specific corporation if

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the non-resident person would, absent the application of this paragraph, be a member of the particular group, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the non-resident person is a member of the particular group solely because it controls, or is a member of a group that controls, another member of the particular group.

Subsection.Text: The portion of paragraph 212.3(16)(a) of the Act before subparagraph (i) is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the business activities carried on by the subject corporation and all other corporations (those other corporations in this subsection and subsection (17) referred to as the “subject subsidiary corporations”) in which the subject corporation has, at the investment time, an equity percentage (as defined in subsection 95(4)) are at the investment time, and are expected to remain, on a collective basis, more closely connected to the business activities carried on in Canada by the CRIC, or by any corporation resident in Canada with which the CRIC does not, at the investment time, deal at arm’s length, than to the business activities carried on by any non-resident person with which the CRIC, at the investment time, does not deal at arm’s length, other than

Subsection.Text: Clauses 212.3(18)(a)(i)(A) and (B) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: each shareholder of the disposing corporation immediately before the investment time is

Subclause.Label: (I)
Subclause.Text: if there is only one parent in respect of the CRIC,

Subsubclause.Text: either the CRIC or a corporation resident in Canada that is, immediately before the investment time, related to the parent, and

at no time that is in the period during which the series of transactions or events that includes the making of the investment occurs and that is before the investment time, dealing at arm’s length (determined without reference to paragraph 251(5)(b)) with the parent or a non-resident person that participates in the series and is, at any time that is in the period and that is before the investment time, related to the parent, and

Subclause.Label: (II)
Subclause.Text: if there is a group of parents in respect of the CRIC,

Subsubclause.Text: either the CRIC or a corporation resident in Canada that is, immediately before the investment time, controlled by the group of parents, and

at all times that are in the period during which the series of transactions or events that includes the making of the investment occurs and that are before the investment time, controlled by the group of parents, or

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: the disposing corporation is

Subclause.Label: (I)
Subclause.Text: if there is only one parent in respect of the CRIC, at no time that is in the period and that is before the investment time, dealing at arm’s length (determined without reference to paragraph 251(5)(b)) with the parent or a non-resident person that participates in the series and is, at any time that is in the period and that is before the investment time, related to the parent, and

Subclause.Label: (II)
Subclause.Text: if there is a group of parents in respect of the CRIC, at all times that are in the period during which the series of transactions or events that includes the making of the investment occurs and that are before the investment time, controlled by the group of parents, or

Subsection.Text: Subparagraph 212.3(18)(a)(ii) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: on an amalgamation described in subsection 87(1) of two or more corporations (each of which is in this subparagraph referred to as a “predecessor corporation”) to form the CRIC if all of the predecessor corporations are, immediately before the amalgamation, related to each other (determined without reference to paragraph 251(5)(b)) and

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: either

Subclause.Label: (I)
Subclause.Text: if there is only one parent in respect of the CRIC, none of the predecessor corporations are, at any time that is in the period during which the series of transactions or events that includes the making of the investment occurs and that is before the investment time, dealing at arm’s length (determined without reference to paragraph 251(5)(b)) with the parent or a non-resident person that participates in the series and is, at any time that is in the period and that is before the investment time, related to the parent, or

Subclause.Label: (II)
Subclause.Text: if there is a group of parents in respect of the CRIC, all of the predecessor corporations are, at all times that are in the period during which the series of transactions or events that includes the making of the investment occurs and that are before the investment time, controlled by the group of parents, or

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: if the condition in clause (A) is not satisfied in respect of a predecessor corporation, each shareholder of that predecessor immediately before the investment time is

Subclause.Label: (I)
Subclause.Text: if there is only one parent in respect of the CRIC,

Subsubclause.Text: either the CRIC or a corporation resident in Canada that is, immediately before the investment time, related to the parent, and

at no time that is in the period and that is before the investment time, dealing at arm’s length (determined without reference to paragraph 251(5)(b)) with the parent or a non-resident person that participates in the series and is, at any time that is in the period and that is before the investment time, related to the parent, and

Subclause.Label: (II)
Subclause.Text: if there is a group of parents in respect of the CRIC,

Subsubclause.Text: either the CRIC or a corporation resident in Canada that is, immediately before the investment time, controlled by the group of parents, and

at all times that are in the period during which the series of transactions or events that includes the making of the investment occurs and that are before the investment time, controlled by the group of parents;

Subsection.Text: Subparagraphs 212.3(18)(c)(i) and (ii) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: from a corporation (in this paragraph referred to as the “disposing corporation”) to which the CRIC is, immediately before the investment time, related (determined without reference to paragraph 251(5)(b)) and

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: each shareholder of the disposing corporation immediately before the investment time is

Subclause.Label: (I)
Subclause.Text: if there is only one parent in respect of the CRIC,

Subsubclause.Text: either the CRIC or a corporation resident in Canada that, immediately before the investment time, is related to the parent, and

at no time that is in the period during which the series of transactions or events that includes the making of the investment occurs and that is before the investment time, dealing at arm’s length (determined without reference to paragraph 251(5)(b)) with the parent or a non-resident person that participates in the series and is, at any time that is in the period and that is before the investment time, related to the parent, and

Subclause.Label: (II)
Subclause.Text: if there is a group of parents in respect of the CRIC,

Subsubclause.Text: either the CRIC or a corporation resident in Canada that is, immediately before the investment time, controlled by the group of parents, and

at all times that are in the period during which the series of transactions or events that includes the making of the investment occurs and that are before the investment time, controlled by the group of parents, or

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: the disposing corporation is

Subclause.Label: (I)
Subclause.Text: if there is only one parent in respect of the CRIC, at no time that is in the period and that is before the investment time, dealing at arm’s length (determined without reference to paragraph 251(5)(b)) with the parent or a non-resident person that participates in the series and is, at any time that is in the period and that is before the investment time, related to the parent, and

Subclause.Label: (II)
Subclause.Text: if there is a group of parents in respect of the CRIC, at all times that are in the period during which the series of transactions or events that includes the making of the investment occurs and that are before the investment time, controlled by the group of parents, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: on an amalgamation described in subsection 87(1) of two or more corporations (each of which is in this subparagraph referred to as a “predecessor corporation”) to form the CRIC, or a corporation of which the CRIC is a shareholder, if all of the predecessor corporations are, immediately before the amalgamation, related to each other (determined without reference to paragraph 251(5)(b)) and

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: either

Subclause.Label: (I)
Subclause.Text: if there is only one parent in respect of the CRIC, none of the predecessor corporations are, at any time that is in the period during which the series of transactions or events that includes the making of the investment occurs and that is before the investment time, dealing at arm’s length (determined without reference to paragraph 251(5)(b)) with the parent or a non-resident person that participates in the series and is, at any time that is in the period and that is before the investment time, related to the parent, or

Subclause.Label: (II)
Subclause.Text: if there is a group of parents in respect of the CRIC, all of the predecessor corporations are, at all times that are in the period during which the series of transactions or events that includes the making of the investment occurs and that are before the investment time, controlled by the group of parents, or

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: if the condition in clause (A) is not satisfied in respect of a predecessor corporation, each shareholder of that predecessor immediately before the investment time is

Subclause.Label: (I)
Subclause.Text: if there is only one parent in respect of the CRIC,

Subsubclause.Text: either the CRIC or a corporation resident in Canada that is, immediately before the investment time, related to the parent, and

at no time that is in the period and that is before the investment time, dealing at arm’s length (determined without reference to paragraph 251(5)(b)) with the parent or a non-resident person that participates in the series and is, at any time that is in the period and that is before the investment time, related to the parent, and

Subclause.Label: (II)
Subclause.Text: if there is a group of parents in respect of the CRIC,

Subsubclause.Text: either the CRIC or a corporation resident in Canada that is, immediately before the investment time, controlled by the group of parents, and

at all times that are in the period during which the series of transactions or events that includes the making of the investment occurs and that are before the investment time, controlled by the group of parents;

Subsection.Text: Subsection 212.3(21) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.MarginalNote: Persons deemed not to be related
Subsection.Text: If it can reasonably be considered that one of the main purposes of one or more transactions or events is to cause two or more persons to be related to each other, or a person or group of persons to control another person, so that, in the absence of this subsection, subsection (2) would not apply because of subsection (18) to an investment in a subject corporation made by a CRIC, those persons are deemed not to be related to each other, or that person or group of persons is deemed not to control that other person, as the case may be, for the purposes of subsection (18).
Section 212.3 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (25):

For the purposes of this section, subsection 17.1(1) (as it applies in respect of a pertinent loan or indebtedness as defined in subsection (11)), paragraph 128.1(1)(c.3) and subsection 219.1(2), and for the purpose of paragraph 251(1)(a) as it applies for the purposes of those provisions,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: in determining, at any time, whether two persons are related to each other or whether any person is controlled by any other person or group of persons, it shall be assumed that

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: each trust is a corporation having a capital stock of a single class of voting shares divided into 100 issued shares, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: each beneficiary under a trust owned at that time the number of issued shares of that class determined by the formula

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the fair market value at that time of the beneficiary’s interest in the trust, and
is the total fair market value at that time of all beneficiaries’ interests in the trust;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: in determining, at any time, the extent to which any person owns shares of the capital stock of a corporation, if at that time a trust resident in Canada owns (determined without reference to this paragraph) shares of the capital stock of the corporation, each beneficiary of the trust is deemed to own, and the trust is deemed not to own, at that time, the shares of each class of the capital stock of the corporation that are owned (determined without reference to this paragraph) by the trust, the number of which is determined by the formula

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the total number of shares of the class of the capital stock of the corporation that are owned (determined without reference to this paragraph) by the trust at that time,
is the fair market value, at that time, of the beneficiary’s interest in the trust, and
is the total fair market value, at that time, of all beneficiaries’ interests in the trust; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: if a beneficiary’s share of the income or capital of a trust depends on the exercise by any person of, or the failure by any person to exercise, any discretionary power, then the amounts determined for A and B in paragraph (a), and for B and C in paragraph (b), in respect of the beneficiary are deemed to be equal to one, unless

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the trust is resident in Canada, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: it cannot reasonably be considered that one of the main reasons for the discretionary power is to avoid or limit the application of paragraph 128.1(1)(c.3) or subsection 212.3(2) or 219.1(2).

Subsection.Text: Subsections (1) to (22) apply in respect of transactions or events that occur after March 18, 2019.
Paragraph 219.1(2)(b) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the other corporation is controlled, at that time, by a non-resident person or a group of non-resident persons not dealing with each other at arm’s length; and

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) applies in respect of transactions or events that occur after March 18, 2019.
The portion of subsection 231.2(1) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Requirement to provide documents or information

Subsection.Text: Notwithstanding any other provision of this Act, the Minister may, subject to subsection (2), for any purpose related to the administration or enforcement of this Act (including the collection of any amount payable under this Act by any person), of a listed international agreement or, for greater certainty, of a tax treaty with another country, by notice sent or served in accordance with subsection (1.1), require that any person provide, within such reasonable time as is stipulated in the notice,
Section 231.2 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (1):
A notice referred to in subsection (1) may be

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: served personally;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: sent by registered or certified mail; or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: sent electronically to a bank or credit union that has provided written consent to receive notices under subsection (1) electronically.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 231.6(2) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Notwithstanding any other provision of this Act, the Minister may, by notice sent or served in accordance with subsection (3.1), require that a person resident in Canada or a non-resident person carrying on business in Canada provide any foreign-based information or document.
Section 231.6 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (3):
A notice referred to in subsection (2) may be

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: served personally;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: sent by registered or certified mail; or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: sent electronically to a bank or credit union that has provided written consent to receive notices under subsection (2) electronically.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 231.6(4) of the English version of the Act is replaced by the following:
The person who is sent or served with a notice of a requirement under subsection (2) may, within 90 days after the notice is sent or served, apply to a judge for a review of the requirement.
Subsection 231.6(6) of the English version of the Act is replaced by the following:

For the purposes of paragraph (5)(c), the requirement to provide the information or document shall not be considered to be unreasonable because the information or document is under the control of or available to a non-resident person that is not controlled by the person who is sent or served with the notice of the requirement under subsection (2) if that person is related to the non-resident person.
Subsection 231.6(8) of the Act is replaced by the following:

If a person fails to comply substantially with a notice sent or served under subsection (2) and if the notice is not set aside by a judge pursuant to subsection (5), any court having jurisdiction in a civil proceeding relating to the administration or enforcement of this Act shall, on motion of the Minister, prohibit the introduction by that person of any foreign-based information or document covered by that notice.

Section.Text: Paragraph 231.8(a) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: where the taxpayer is sent or served with a notice of a requirement under subsection 231.2(1), the period of time between the day on which an application for judicial review in respect of the requirement is made and the day on which the application is finally disposed of; and

Subsection.Text: Paragraphs 241(3.4)(a) and (b) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the names of each organization in respect of which an individual can be entitled to a deduction under subsection 118.02(2);

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: information relating to the eligibility, for the deduction under subsection 118.02(2), of subscriptions offered by organizations referred to in paragraph (a); and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the start and, if applicable, end of the period in which paragraph (a) or (b) applies in respect of any particular organization or subscription.

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) is deemed to have come into force on January 1, 2020.

Section.Text: Section 244 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (6):

Subsection.Text: If, by this Act or a regulation, provision is made for sending a notice to a person electronically, an affidavit of an officer of the Canada Revenue Agency sworn before a commissioner or other person authorized to take affidavits, shall, in the absence of proof to the contrary, be received as evidence of the sending and of the notice if the affidavit sets out that

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the officer has knowledge of the facts in the particular case;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the notice was sent electronically to the person on a named day; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the officer identifies as exhibits attached to the affidavit copies of

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: an electronic message confirming the notice has been sent to the person, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the notice.

Subsection.Text: The portion of subsection 247(2) of the Act after paragraph (b) and before paragraph (c) is replaced by the following:

ContinuedSectionSubsection.Text: any amounts (in subsection (2.1) referred to as the “initial amounts”) that would be determined for the purposes of applying the provisions of this Act (if this Act were read without reference to this section and section 245) in respect of the taxpayer or the partnership for a taxation year or fiscal period shall be adjusted (in this section referred to as an “adjustment”) to the quantum or nature of the amounts (in subsection (2.1) referred to as the “adjusted amounts”) that would have been determined if,

Subsection.Text: Section 247 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (2):
For the purpose of applying subsection (2) in the context of the other provisions of this Act, the following order is to be applied:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: first determine each of the initial amounts;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: then make the adjustments, if any, to each of the initial amounts; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: then apply each of the provisions of this Act (other than subsection (2) and, for greater certainty, including section 245) using the adjusted amounts.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 247(8) of the Act is repealed.
Subsections (1) to (3) apply to taxation years that begin after March 18, 2019.
Subparagraph (b)(i) of the definition derivative forward agreement in subsection 248(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: revenue, income or cashflow in respect of the property over the term of the agreement, changes in the fair market value of the property over the term of the agreement, or any similar criteria in respect of the property unless

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: the property is

Subclause.Label: (I)
Subclause.Text: a Canadian security (as defined in subsection 39(6)), or

Subclause.Label: (II)
Subclause.Text: an interest in a partnership the fair market value of which is derived, in whole or in part, from a Canadian security,

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: the agreement is an agreement to acquire property from

Subclause.Label: (I)
Subclause.Text: a tax-indifferent investor, or

Subclause.Label: (II)
Subclause.Text: a financial institution (as defined in subsection 142.2(1)), and

Clause.Label: (C)
Clause.Text: it can reasonably be considered that one of the main purposes of the series of transactions or events, or any transaction or event in the series, of which the agreement is part is for all or any portion of the capital gain on a disposition (other than a disposition by the seller to the taxpayer under the agreement) of a Canadian security referred to in clause (A) — as part of the same series of transactions or events — to be attributable to amounts paid or payable on the Canadian security by the issuer of the Canadian security during the term of the agreement as

Subclause.Label: (I)
Subclause.Text: interest,

Subclause.Label: (II)
Subclause.Text: dividends, or

Subclause.Label: (III)
Subclause.Text: income of a trust other than income paid out of the taxable capital gains of the trust,

Subsection.Text: The portion of subparagraph (a)(v) of the definition qualified Canadian journalism organization in subsection 248(1) of the Act before clause (A) is replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (v)
Subparagraph.Text: it is engaged in the production of original news content, which

Subsection.Text: Subparagraph (a)(vii) of the definition qualified Canadian journalism organization in subsection 248(1) of the Act is amended by adding “or” at the end of clause (A), by replacing “or” at the end of clause (B) with “and” and by repealing clause (C).
Paragraph (b) of the definition tax-indifferent investor in subsection 248(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a non-resident person, other than a person to which all amounts paid or credited under a derivative forward agreement, a synthetic equity arrangement or a specified synthetic equity arrangement, as the case may be, may reasonably be attributed to the business carried on by the person in Canada through a permanent establishment (as defined in section 8201 of the Income Tax Regulations) in Canada,

Subsection.Text: The definition zero-emission vehicle in subsection 248(1) of the Act is amended by striking out “and” at the end of paragraph (b) and by replacing paragraph (c) with the following:

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: does not meet any of the following conditions:

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: it is a vehicle in respect of which the taxpayer has, at any time, made an election under subsection 1103(2j) of the Income Tax Regulations,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: it is a vehicle in respect of which assistance has been paid by the Government of Canada under a prescribed program, and

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: if the vehicle was acquired before March 2, 2020, either

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: it has been used, or acquired for use, for any purpose before it was acquired by the taxpayer, or

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: it is a vehicle in respect of which an amount has been deducted under paragraph 20(1)(a) or subsection 20(16) by another person or partnership, and

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: would be an accelerated investment incentive property of the taxpayer if subsection 1104(4) of the Income Tax Regulations were read without its exclusions for property included in Class 54 or Class 55 of Schedule II to those Regulations. (véhicule zéro émission)

Subsection.Text: Subsection 248(1) of the Act is amended by adding the following in alphabetical order:

Definition.Text: advanced life deferred annuity has the meaning assigned by subsection 146.5(1); (rente viagère différée à un âge avancé)

Subsection.Text: Subsection 248(1) of the Act is amended by adding the following in alphabetical order:

Definition.Text: fully collateralized arrangement means a securities lending arrangement or a specified securities lending arrangement if, throughout the term of the arrangement, the borrower

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: has provided the lender under the arrangement with money in an amount of, or securities described in paragraph (c) of the definition qualified security in subsection 260(1) that have a fair market value of, not less than 95% of the fair market value of the security that is transferred or lent under the arrangement, and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: is entitled to enjoy, directly or indirectly, the benefits of all or substantially all income derived from, and opportunity for gain in respect of, the money or securities provided; (mécanisme entièrement garanti)

Definition.Text: specified securities lending arrangement has the same meaning as in subsection 260(1); (mécanisme de prêt de valeurs mobilières déterminé)

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) is deemed to have come into force on March 19, 2019. However, it does not apply before 2020 in respect of

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: an agreement that is entered into after the final settlement of another derivative forward agreement (in this paragraph referred to as the “prior agreement”) if

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: having regard to the source of the funds used to purchase the property to be sold under the agreement, it is reasonable to conclude that the agreement is a continuation of the prior agreement,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the terms of the agreement and the prior agreement are substantially similar,

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: the final settlement date under the agreement is before 2020,

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: subsection (1) does not apply to the prior agreement, and

Subparagraph.Label: (v)
Subparagraph.Text: the notional amount of the agreement is at all times less than or equal to the amount determined by the formula

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the notional amount of the agreement when it is entered into,
is the total of all amounts each of which is an increase in the notional amount of the agreement, at or before that time, that is attributable to the underlying interest,
is the amount of the taxpayer’s cash on hand immediately before March 19, 2019 that was committed, before March 19, 2019, to be invested under the agreement,

is the total of all amounts each of which is an increase, at or before that time, in the notional amount of the agreement that is attributable to the final settlement of another derivative forward agreement if subsection (1) does not apply to the other agreement,
is the lesser of

FormulaParagraph.Label: (A)
FormulaParagraph.Text: either

FormulaParagraph.Label: (I)
FormulaParagraph.Text: if the prior agreement was entered into before March 19, 2019, the amount, if any, by which the amount determined under subparagraph (i) of the description of F in paragraph (b) for the prior agreement immediately before it was finally settled exceeds the total determined under subparagraph (ii) of the description of F in paragraph (b) for the prior agreement immediately before it was finally settled, or

FormulaParagraph.Label: (II)
FormulaParagraph.Text: in any other case, the amount, if any, by which the amount determined under this clause for the prior agreement immediately before it was finally settled exceeds the total determined under clause (B) for the prior agreement immediately before it was finally settled, and

FormulaParagraph.Label: (B)
FormulaParagraph.Text: the total of all amounts each of which is an increase in the notional amount of the agreement before 2020 that is not otherwise described in this formula,

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the total of all amounts each of which is a decrease in the notional amount of the agreement, at or before that time, that is attributable to the underlying interest, and
is the total of all amounts each of which is the amount of a partial settlement of the agreement, at or before that time, to the extent that it is not reinvested in the agreement; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: an agreement that is entered into before March 19, 2019, unless at any time on or after March 19, 2019, the notional amount of the agreement exceeds the amount determined by the formula

Formula.FormulaText: (A + B + C + D + E + F) – (G + H)

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the notional amount of the agreement immediately before March 19, 2019,

is the total of all amounts each of which is an increase in the notional amount of the agreement, on or after March 19, 2019 and at or before that time, that is attributable to the underlying interest,
is the amount of the taxpayer’s cash on hand immediately before March 19, 2019 that was committed, before March 19, 2019, to be invested under the agreement,

is the amount, if any, of an increase, on or after March 19, 2019 and at or before that time, in the notional amount of the agreement as a consequence of the exercise of an over-allotment option granted before March 19, 2019,

is the total of all amounts each of which is an increase, on or after March 19, 2019 and at or before that time, in the notional amount of the agreement that is attributable to the final settlement of another derivative forward agreement if subsection (1) does not apply to the other agreement,
is the lesser of

FormulaParagraph.Label: (i)
FormulaParagraph.Text: 5% of the notional amount of the agreement immediately before March 19, 2019, and

FormulaParagraph.Label: (ii)
FormulaParagraph.Text: the total of all amounts each of which is an increase in the notional amount of the agreement on or after March 19, 2019 and before 2020 that is not otherwise described in this formula,

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the total of all amounts each of which is a decrease in the notional amount of the agreement, on or after March 19, 2019 and at or before that time, that is attributable to the underlying interest, and

is the total of all amounts each of which is the amount of a partial settlement of the agreement, on or after March 19, 2019 and at or before that time, to the extent that it is not reinvested in the agreement.

Subsection.Text: For the purposes of subsection (8), the notional amount of a derivative forward agreement at any time is the fair market value at that time of the property that would be acquired under the agreement if the agreement were finally settled at that time.
Subsections (2) and (3) are deemed to have come into force on January 1, 2019.
Subsections (4) and (7) are deemed to have come into force on March 19, 2019.
Subsection (5) is deemed to have come into force on March 2, 2020.
Subsection (6) is deemed to have come into force on January 1, 2020.
Paragraph 250(1)(f) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: was at any time in the year a child of, and dependent for support on, an individual to whom paragraph (b), (c), (d) or (d.1) applies and the person’s income for the year did not exceed the amount determined for F in subsection 118(1.1) for the year; or

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) applies to the 2020 and subsequent taxation years.
Subsection 252(3) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.MarginalNote: Extended meaning of spouse and former spouse
Subsection.Text: For the purposes of paragraph 56(1)(b), section 56.1, paragraphs 60(b) and (j), section 60.1, subsections 70(6) and (6.1), 73(1) and (5) and 104(4) and (5.1), the definition pre-1972 spousal trust in subsection 108(1), subsection 146(16), the definition survivor in subsection 146.2(1), subparagraph 146.3(2)(f)(iv), subsection 146.3(14), section 146.5, subsections 147(19) and 147.3(5) and (7), section 147.5, subsections 148(8.1) and (8.2), the definition qualifying transfer in subsection 207.01(1), and subsections 210(1) and 248(22) and (23), spouse and former spouse of a particular individual include another individual who is a party to a void or voidable marriage with the particular individual.
Subsection (1) is deemed to have come into force on January 1, 2020.
Section 260 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (1.1):
For the purposes of subsections (8), (8.1), (8.2), (8.3) and (9.1) and 212(2.1) and (3), in respect of a specified securities lending arrangement,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a reference to a borrower includes a transferee; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a reference to a lender includes a transferor.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 260(8) of the Act is replaced by the following:
For the purpose of Part XIII, any amount paid or credited under a securities lending arrangement or a specified securities lending arrangement by or on behalf of the borrower to the lender

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: as an SLA compensation payment in respect of a security that is not a qualified trust unit is, subject to paragraph (c), deemed

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: to the extent of the amount of the interest paid in respect of the security, to be a payment made by the borrower to the lender of interest, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: to the extent of the amount of the dividend paid in respect of the security, to be a payment made by the borrower, as a corporation, to the lender of a dividend payable on the security;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: as an SLA compensation payment in respect of a security that is a qualified trust unit, is deemed, to the extent of the amount of the underlying payment to which the SLA compensation payment relates, to be an amount paid by the trust and having the same character and composition as the underlying payment;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: as an SLA compensation payment is deemed to be a payment of interest made by the borrower to the lender, if

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the security that is transferred or lent to the borrower under the arrangement is a share of a class of the capital stock of a non-resident corporation,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the borrower and the lender are not dealing at arm’s length, and

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: the arrangement is not a fully collateralized arrangement; and

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: as, on account of, in lieu of payment of or in satisfaction of, a fee for the use of the security is deemed to be a payment of interest made by the borrower to the lender.

Subsection.Text: The portion of subsection 260(8.1) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

For the purpose of paragraph (8)(d), if under a securities lending arrangement or a specified securities lending arrangement the borrower has at any time provided the lender with money, either as collateral or consideration for the security, and the borrower does not, under the arrangement, pay or credit a reasonable amount to the lender as, on account of, in lieu of payment of or in satisfaction of, a fee for the use of the security, the borrower is deemed to have, at the time that an identical or substantially identical security is or can reasonably be expected to be transferred or returned to the lender, paid to the lender under the arrangement an amount as a fee for the use of the security equal to the amount, if any, by which
Subsection 260(8.2) of the Act is replaced by the following:

In applying subparagraph (8)(a)(i), if a securities lending arrangement or specified securities lending arrangement is a fully collateralized arrangement, any SLA compensation payment deemed to be a payment made by the borrower to the lender of interest is deemed for the purposes of any tax treaty to be payable on the security.

In applying subparagraph (8)(a)(ii), if the security is a share of a class of the capital stock of a corporation resident in Canada (in this subsection referred to as the “Canadian share”), for the purposes of determining the rate of tax that Canada may impose on a dividend because of the dividend article of a tax treaty,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: any SLA compensation payment deemed to be a payment made by the borrower to the lender of a dividend is deemed to be paid by the issuer of the Canadian share and not by the borrower;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the lender is deemed to be the beneficial owner of the Canadian share; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the shares of the capital stock of the issuer owned by the lender are deemed to give it less than 10% of the votes that could be cast at an annual meeting of the shareholders of the issuer and have less than 10% of the fair market value of all of the issued and outstanding shares of the capital stock of the issuer, if

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the securities lending arrangement or the specified securities lending arrangement is not a fully collateralized arrangement, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the borrower and the lender are not dealing at arm’s length.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 260(9.1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

For the purpose of Part XIII, if the lender under a securities lending arrangement or a specified securities lending arrangement is not dealing at arm’s length with either the borrower under the arrangement or the issuer of the security that is transferred or lent under the arrangement, or both, and subsection (8) deems an amount to be a payment of interest by a person to the lender, the lender is deemed, in respect of that payment, not to be dealing at arm’s length with that person.
Subsection (1) is deemed to have come into force on March 19, 2019.

Subsections (2) to (5) apply in respect of amounts paid or credited as SLA compensation payments after March 18, 2019. However, subsections (2) to (5) do not apply in respect of amounts paid or credited as SLA compensation payments after March 18, 2019 and before October 2019, if they are pursuant to a written arrangement entered into before March 19, 2019.

Section.Text: Subsection 99(1) of the Excise Tax Act is replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Provision of documents may be required

Subsection.Text: Subject to section 102.1, the Minister may, for any purpose related to the administration or enforcement of this Act, or of a listed international agreement, by a notice served or sent in accordance with subsection (1.1), require that any person provide any book, record, writing or other document or any information or further information within any reasonable time that may be stipulated in the notice.
A notice referred to in subsection (1) may be

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: served personally;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: sent by registered or certified mail; or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: sent electronically to a bank or credit union (as defined in subsection 123(1)) that has provided written consent to receive notices under subsection (1) electronically.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 102.1(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

The Minister shall not serve or send a notice under subsection 99(1) with respect to an unnamed person or a group of unnamed persons unless the Minister has been authorized to do so under subsection (2).
The portion of subsection 102.1(2) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

A judge of the Federal Court may, on application by the Minister and subject to any conditions that the judge considers appropriate, authorize the Minister to serve or send a notice under subsection 99(1) with respect to an unnamed person or a group of unnamed persons if the judge is satisfied by information on oath that
Paragraph 102.1(2)(b) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the notice would be served or sent in order to verify compliance by the person or group with any duty or obligation of that person or of persons in that group under this Act.

Section.Text: Section 105 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (2):

Subsection.Text: If, under this Act or a regulation made under this Act, provision is made for sending a notice to a person electronically, an affidavit of an officer of the Agency, sworn before a commissioner or other person authorized to take affidavits, is evidence of the sending and of the notice if the affidavit sets out that

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the officer has knowledge of the facts in the particular case;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the notice was sent electronically to the person on a named day; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the officer identifies as exhibits annexed to the affidavit copies of

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: an electronic message confirming that the notice has been sent to the person, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the notice.

Subsection.Text: The portion of subsection 289(1) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Subsection 292(2) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Despite any other provision of this Part, the Minister may, by a notice served or sent in accordance with subsection (3.1), require a person resident in Canada or a non-resident person that carries on business in Canada to provide any foreign-based information or document.
Section 292 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (3):
A notice referred to in subsection (2) may be

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: served personally;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: sent by registered or certified mail; or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: sent electronically to a bank or credit union that has provided written consent to receive notices under subsection (2) electronically.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 292(4) of the English version of the Act is replaced by the following:

If a person is served or sent a notice of a requirement under subsection (2), the person may, within 90 days after the day on which the notice is served or sent, apply to a judge for a review of the requirement.
Subsection 292(6) of the English version of the Act is replaced by the following:

For the purposes of subsection (5), a requirement to provide information or a document shall not be considered to be unreasonable because the information or document is under the control of or available to a non-resident person that is not controlled by the person on which the notice of the requirement under subsection (2) is served, or to which that notice is sent, if that person is related to the non-resident person.
Subsection 292(8) of the English version of the Act is replaced by the following:

If a person fails to comply substantially with a notice served or sent under subsection (2) and if the notice is not set aside under subsection (5), any court having jurisdiction in a civil proceeding relating to the administration or enforcement of this Part shall, on motion of the Minister, prohibit the introduction by that person of any foreign-based information or document covered by that notice.

Subsection.Text: The definition remuneration in subsection 100(1) of the Income Tax Regulations is amended by striking out “or” at the end of paragraph (n), by adding “or” at the end of paragraph (o) and by adding the following after paragraph (o):

Paragraph.Label: (p)
Paragraph.Text: an amount that is required by paragraph 56(1)(z.5) of the Act to be included in computing a taxpayer’s income; (rémunération)

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) is deemed to have come into force on January 1, 2020.
Subparagraph (b)(i) of the description of B in subsection 103.1(2) of the Regulations is replaced by the following:

FormulaParagraph.Label: (i)
FormulaParagraph.Text: the amount determined for F in subsection 118(1.1) of the Act for the taxation year, and

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) applies to the 2020 and subsequent taxation years.
The Regulations are amended by adding the following after section 215:
In this section, designated entity means

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: an administrator of a registered pension plan;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: an administrator of a pooled registered pension plan;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: an issuer of a registered retirement savings plan;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: a carrier of a registered retirement income fund; and

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: a trustee of a deferred profit sharing plan.

Subsection.Text: A designated entity that transfers an amount to acquire an advanced life deferred annuity for an individual shall make an information return in prescribed form in respect of the year in which the transfer was made.
A licensed annuities provider shall make an information return in prescribed form in respect of a year in which

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a payment is made that is required by section 146.5 of the Act to be included in computing the income of a taxpayer; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a refund described in paragraph (g) of the definition advanced life deferred annuity in subsection 146.5(1) of the Act was received by a taxpayer.

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) is deemed to have come into force on January 1, 2020.

Paragraph 1100(1)(a) of the Regulations is amended by striking out “and” at the end of subparagraph (xl), by adding “and” at the end of subparagraph (xli) and by adding the following after subparagraph (xli):

Subparagraph.Label: (xlii)
Subparagraph.Text: of Class 56, 30 per cent,

Subsection.Text: The portion of the description of A in subsection 1100(2) of the Regulations before subparagraph (a)(i) is replaced by the following:

FormulaParagraph.Label: (a)
FormulaParagraph.Text: if the property is not included in paragraph (1)(v) or in any of Classes 12, 13, 14, 15, 43.1, 43.2, 53, 54, 55 and 56 or in Class 43 in the circumstances described in paragraph (d),

FormulaParagraph.Label: (ii)

FormulaParagraph.Label: (iii)
FormulaParagraph.Text: 1/10, for property that became available for use by the taxpayer after 2025,

Subsection.Text: Subparagraph (d)(iii) of the description of A in subsection 1100(2) of the Regulations is replaced by the following:

FormulaParagraph.Label: (iii)

FormulaParagraph.Label: (iv)
FormulaParagraph.Text: 1/10, for property included in Class 53 that became available for use by the taxpayer after 2025,

FormulaParagraph.Label: (e)
FormulaParagraph.Text: if the class is Class 54 or Class 56,

Subsection.Text: The description of D in subsection 1100(2) of the Regulations is replaced by the following:

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the total of all amounts, if any, each of which is an amount included in the description of A in the definition undepreciated capital cost in subsection 13(21) of the Act in respect of property of the class that became available for use by the taxpayer in the taxation year and that is accelerated investment incentive property or property included in any of Classes 54 to 56, and

Subsection.Text: Subparagraph (b)(ii) of the description of F in subsection 1100(2) of the Regulations is replaced by the following:

FormulaParagraph.Label: (ii)
FormulaParagraph.Text: property included in any of Classes 13, 14, 15, 23, 24, 27, 29, 34, 52 and 54 to 56,

Subsection.Text: Subsection 1100(2.02) of the Regulations is replaced by the following:
For the purposes of subsection (2), in respect of property of a class in Schedule II that is accelerated investment incentive property of a taxpayer solely because of subparagraph 1104(4)(b)(i),

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: amounts incurred by any person or partnership in respect of the property are not to be included in determining the amount for D in subsection (2) in respect of the class

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: if the amounts are incurred before November 21, 2018, unless

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: the property was acquired after November 20, 2018 by a person or partnership from another person or partnership (referred to in this subparagraph as the “transferee” and the “transferor”, respectively),

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: the transferee was either

Subclause.Label: (I)
Subclause.Text: the taxpayer, or

Subclause.Label: (II)
Subclause.Text: a person or partnership that does not deal at arm’s length with the taxpayer, and

Clause.Label: (C)
Clause.Text: the transferor

Subclause.Label: (I)
Subclause.Text: dealt at arm’s length with the transferee, and

Subclause.Label: (II)
Subclause.Text: held the property as inventory, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: if the amounts are incurred after November 20, 2018 and amounts are deemed to have been deducted under paragraph 20(1)(a) or subsection 20(16), in respect of those amounts incurred, under paragraph 1104(4.1)(b); and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: any amount excluded from the amount determined for D in subsection (2) in respect of the class because of paragraph (a) is to be included in determining the amount for F in subsection (2) in respect of the class, unless no amount in respect of the property would be so included if the property were not accelerated investment incentive property of the taxpayer.

Subsection.Text: Subsections (1), (2) and (5) to (7) are deemed to have come into force on March 2, 2020.
Subsections (3), (4) and (8) apply in respect of property acquired after November 20, 2018.
Subsection 1102(14.13) of the Regulations is replaced by the following:
Subsection (14) does not apply to an acquisition of property by a taxpayer from a person in respect of which the property was included in any of Classes 54 to 56.
Subsection 1102(20.1) of the Regulations is replaced by the following:

For the purposes of subsections 1100(2.02) and 1104(4), a particular person or partnership and another person or partnership shall be considered not to be dealing at arm’s length with each other in respect of the acquisition or ownership of a property if, in the absence of this subsection, they would be considered to be dealing at arm’s length with each other and it may reasonably be considered that the principal purpose of any transaction or event, or a series of transactions or events, is to cause

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the property to qualify as accelerated investment incentive property; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the particular person or partnership and the other person or partnership to satisfy the condition in subclause 1100(2.02)(a)(i)(C)(I).

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) is deemed to have come into force on March 2, 2020.
Subsection (2) applies in respect of property acquired after July 30, 2019.
Subsection 1103(2j) of the Regulations is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Label: (2j)
Subsection.Text: A taxpayer may, in its return of income filed with the Minister on or before its filing-due date for the taxation year in which a property is acquired, elect not to include the property in any of Classes 54 to 56 in Schedule II, as the case may be.
Subsection (1) is deemed to have come into force on March 2, 2020.
The portion of subsection 1104(4) of the Regulations before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:
For the purposes of this Part and Schedules II to VI, accelerated investment incentive property means property of a taxpayer (other than property included in any of Classes 54 to 56) that
Subparagraph 1104(4)(b)(i) of the Regulations is replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the property is not a property in respect of which an amount has been deducted under paragraph 20(1)(a) or subsection 20(16) of the Act by any person or partnership for a taxation year ending before the time the property was acquired by the taxpayer, or

Subsection.Text: Section 1104 of the Regulations is amended by adding the following after subsection (4):

For the purpose of subparagraph (4)(b)(i), if the capital cost to a taxpayer of a depreciable property (referred to in this subsection as the “single property”) includes amounts incurred at different times, then amounts deducted under paragraph 20(1)(a) or subsection 20(16) of the Act in respect of the single property are deemed to have been deducted in respect of a separate property that is not part of the single property to the extent the deducted amounts can reasonably be considered to be in respect of amounts

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: incurred before November 21, 2018; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: incurred after November 20, 2018, if any portion of the single property is considered to have become available for use before the time the single property is first used for the purpose of earning income.

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) is deemed to have come into force on March 2, 2020.
Subsections (2) and (3) apply in respect of property acquired after November 20, 2018.
Subparagraph 8502(e)(i) of the Regulations is amended by striking out “and” at the end of clause (A) and by adding the following after clause (B):

Clause.Label: (C)
Clause.Text: in the case of benefits provided under a money purchase provision in accordance with paragraph 8506(1)(e.2), the benefits may begin to be paid not later than the later of

Subclause.Label: (I)
Subclause.Text: the end of the calendar year in which the member attains 71 years of age, and

Subclause.Label: (II)
Subclause.Text: the end of the calendar year in which a transfer was made from the member’s account to acquire rights under the VPLA fund, and

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) is deemed to have come into force on January 1, 2020.
The portion of clause 8503(3)(a)(v)(A) of the Regulations before subclause (I) is replaced by the following:

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: unless the provision is a provision of an individual pension plan,

Subsection.Text: The portion of subparagraph 8503(3)(a)(v.1) of the Regulations before clause (A) is replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (v.1)
Subparagraph.Text: unless the provision is a provision of an individual pension plan, a portion — determined by reference to the proportion of property that has been transferred, as described in clause (B) — of a period in respect of which

Subsection.Text: Subparagraph 8503(3)(a)(vi) of the Regulations is replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (vi)
Subparagraph.Text: unless the provision is a provision of an individual pension plan, a period throughout which the member was employed in Canada by a former employer where the period was an eligibility period for the participation of the member in another registered pension plan, and

Subsection.Text: Subsections (1) to (3) are deemed to have come into force on March 19, 2019. However, subsections (1) to (3) do not apply to a period that was pensionable service (as defined in subsection 8500(1) of the Regulations) in respect of a member under a defined benefit provision of an individual pension plan before March 19, 2019.
The portion of paragraph 8506(1)(e.1) of the Regulations before subparagraph (i) is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (e.1)
Paragraph.Text: retirement benefits (in this paragraph referred to as “variable benefits”), other than benefits permissible under any of paragraphs (a) to (e) and (e.2), provided to a member and, after the death of the member, to one or more beneficiaries of the member if

Subsection.Text: Subsection 8506(1) of the Regulations is amended by adding the following after paragraph (e.1):

Paragraph.Label: (e.2)
Paragraph.Text: retirement benefits (referred to in this paragraph as “VPLA benefits”), other than benefits permissible under any of paragraphs (a) to (e.1), provided to a member and, after the death of the member, to one or more beneficiaries of the member if

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the VPLA benefits are paid from a VPLA fund,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the VPLA benefits are provided to the member (or, after the death of the member, to one or more beneficiaries of the member) because of a transfer of one or more amounts from the member’s account to the VPLA fund,

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: each VPLA benefit is any of the following:

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: a retirement benefit described in any of paragraphs (b) to (e), (g) and (i),

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: in the case of the wind-up of the VPLA fund, a payment described in paragraph (h), and

Clause.Label: (C)
Clause.Text: a retirement benefit that would be described in paragraph (a) if its subparagraph (ii) read as follows:

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the benefits are adjusted annually, after they commence to be paid, in whole or in part to reflect

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: increases in the Consumer Price Index, as published by Statistics Canada under the authority of the Statistics Act, or

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: increases at a rate specified under the terms of the plan not exceeding 2% per annum;

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: the VPLA benefits are increased or decreased to the extent that the following differ materially from the actuarial assumptions used to determine the VPLA benefits:

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: the amount or rate of return earned by the VPLA fund, or

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: the rate of mortality of the members and beneficiaries who are entitled to receive the VPLA benefits;

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 8506(2)(g) of the Regulations is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: retirement benefits (other than benefits permissible under paragraph (1)(e.1) or (e.2)) under the provision are provided by means of annuities that are purchased from a licensed annuities provider;

Subsection.Text: Section 8506 of the Regulations is amended by adding the following after subsection (12):
For the purposes of paragraph (1)(e.2) and clause 8502(e)(i)(C), a VPLA fund under a money purchase provision of a pension plan is an arrangement that meets the following conditions:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: no amounts are contributed to the arrangement other than amounts that are transferred from accounts of the members of the plan;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the arrangement has at least 10 members at the time it is established and, at all times after it is established, it is reasonable to expect that the arrangement will have at least 10 members on an ongoing basis; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: no benefit may be paid from the arrangement other than retirement benefits described in subparagraph (1)(e.2)(iii).

Subsection.Text: Subsections (1) to (4) are deemed to have come into force on January 1, 2020.
Subsection 8510(7) of the Regulations is amended by striking out “and” at the end of paragraph (a), by adding “and” at the end of paragraph (b) and by adding the following after paragraph (b):

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: no contributions are made

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: to the plan with respect to a member at any time after the end of the calendar year in which the member attains 71 years of age, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: to a defined benefit provision of the plan with respect to a member during a period (other than a qualifying period, as defined in subsection 8503(16)) in which the member is in receipt of retirement benefits from a defined benefit provision of the plan.

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) applies in respect of contributions made pursuant to any collective bargaining agreement entered into after 2019, except that it does not apply in respect of contributions made on or before the date the agreement is entered into.

Section.Text: Section 8901.2 of the Regulations is replaced by the following:

The amount determined by regulation in respect of a qualifying entity for the purposes of clause (b)(iv)(B) of the description of A in subsection 125.7(2) of the Act for a week in a qualifying period is

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: for the seventh qualifying period and the eighth qualifying period, the greater of

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the amount determined for the week under subparagraph (a)(i) of the description of A in subsection 125.7(2) of the Act, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the amount determined for the week under subparagraph (a)(ii) of the description of A in subsection 125.7(2) of the Act;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: for the ninth qualifying period and the tenth qualifying period, the greater of

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the lesser of

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: 55% of baseline remuneration (as defined in subsection 125.7(1) of the Act) in respect of the eligible employee determined for that week, and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: for any of the eleventh qualifying period to the nineteenth qualifying period, the greater of

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the lesser of

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: 55% of baseline remuneration (as defined in subsection 125.7(1) of the Act) in respect of the eligible employee determined for that week, and

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: for the twentieth qualifying period and any subsequent qualifying period, nil.

Section.Text: Schedule II to the Regulations is amended by adding the following after Class 55:

Provision.Text: Property that is acquired, and becomes available for use, by a taxpayer after March 1, 2020 and before 2028, if the property

Provision.Label: (a)
Provision.Text: is either

Provision.Label: (i)
Provision.Text: automotive equipment (other than a motor vehicle) that is fully electric or powered by hydrogen, or

Provision.Label: (ii)
Provision.Text: an addition or alteration made by the taxpayer to automotive equipment (other than a motor vehicle) to the extent it causes the automotive equipment to become fully electric or powered by hydrogen; and

Provision.Label: (b)
Provision.Text: would be accelerated investment incentive property of the taxpayer if subsection 1104(4) were read without its exclusion for property included in Class 56.

Subsection.Text: Paragraph (b) of the definition montant de retenue in section 1 of the French version of the Canada Disability Savings Regulations is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: b)
Paragraph.Text: dans les autres cas, le montant total des subventions et des bons qui ont été versés dans un REEI au cours des dix années précédant ce moment, déduction faite du montant de toute subvention ou de tout bon versé au cours de cette période qui a été remboursé au ministre. (assistance holdback amount)

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) is deemed to have come into force on January 1, 2021.
The portion of subsection 5(1) of the Regulations before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:
Subject to section 5.1, an issuer of an RDSP shall repay to the Minister, within the period set out in the issuer agreement, the amount referred to in subsection (2) if
Subsection 5(1) of the Regulations is amended by adding “or” at the end of paragraph (b) and by repealing paragraph (c).
Subsection 5(3) of the Regulations is replaced by the following:

Despite subsections (1) and (2), if the beneficiary of an RDSP that is a specified disability savings plan dies, the issuer of the RDSP shall repay to the Minister, within the period set out in the issuer agreement, any portion of an amount paid into the RDSP as a grant or bond within the 10-year period preceding the time of the death that remains in the RDSP at that time.
This section does not apply if the event described in subsection (1) or (3) occurs after the calendar year in which the beneficiary attains 59 years of age.
Subsections (1) to (3) are deemed to have come into force on January 1, 2021.
The portion of section 5.1 of the Regulations before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

Section.Text: If an event described in paragraph 5(1)(a), (b) or (d) occurs while the beneficiary of an RDSP is no longer a DTC-eligible individual, the issuer of the RDSP shall repay to the Minister, within the period set out in the issuer agreement, the lesser of

Subsection.Text: The description of A in paragraph 5.1(b) of the Regulations is replaced by the following:

FormulaDefinition.Text: is

FormulaParagraph.Label: (i)
FormulaParagraph.Text: if the event occurs before the calendar year in which the beneficiary attains 51 years of age, the total amount of grants and bonds paid into the RDSP within the 10-year period before the day on which the beneficiary ceased to be a DTC-eligible individual, less any portion of that amount that was repaid to the Minister within that period,

FormulaParagraph.Label: (ii)
FormulaParagraph.Text: if the event occurs after the calendar year in which the beneficiary attains 50 years of age but before the calendar year in which they attain 60 years of age and the beneficiary ceased to be a DTC-eligible individual before the calendar year in which they attained 50 years of age, the total amount of grants and bonds paid into the RDSP within the period (expressed in number of years) determined by the following formula that ended before the day on which the beneficiary ceased to be a DTC-eligible individual, less any portion of that amount that was repaid to the Minister within that period:

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the beneficiary’s age on — or the age that they would have attained by — December 31 of the calendar year in which the event occurs,

FormulaParagraph.Label: (iii)
